   584→                "message" to "Review submitted successfully"
   585→            ))
   586→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   587→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   588→                "error" to "Bad Request",
   589→                "message" to (e.message ?: "Cannot submit review")
   590→            ))
   591→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   592→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   593→                "error" to "Bad Request",
   594→                "message" to (e.message ?: "Invalid request")
   595→            ))
   596→        } catch (e: NoSuchElementException) {
   597→            return HttpResponse.notFound(mapOf(
   598→                "error" to "Not Found",
   599→                "message" to (e.message ?: "Invalid token or snapshot")
   600→            ))
   601→        }
   602→    }
   603→
   604→    /**
   605→     * GET /api/alignment/review/{token} - Get reviewer info and requirements (via token)
   606→     */
   607→    @Get("/alignment/review/{token}")
   608→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   609→    fun getReviewPageData(@PathVariable token: String): HttpResponse<Map<String, Any>> {
   610→        try {
   611→            val reviewer = alignmentService.getReviewerByToken(token)
   612→            val session = reviewer.session
   613→            val snapshots = alignmentService.getSnapshotsForSession(session.id!!)
   614→            val existingReviews = alignmentService.getReviewsByReviewer(reviewer.id!!)
   615→                .associateBy { it.snapshot.id }
   616→
   617→            return HttpResponse.ok(mapOf(
   618→                "reviewer" to mapOf(
   619→                    "id" to reviewer.id,
   620→                    "username" to reviewer.user.username,
   621→                    "status" to reviewer.status.name,
   622→                    "reviewedCount" to reviewer.reviewedCount
   623→                ),
   624→                "session" to mapOf(
   625→                    "id" to session.id,
   626→                    "status" to session.status.name,
   627→                    "releaseName" to session.release.name,
   628→                    "releaseVersion" to session.release.version,
   629→                    "changedCount" to session.changedRequirementsCount,
   630→                    "startedAt" to session.startedAt?.toString()
   631→                ),
   632→                "snapshots" to snapshots.map { snapshot ->
   633→                    val existingReview = existingReviews[snapshot.id]
   634→                    mapOf(
   635→                        "id" to snapshot.id,
   636→                        "requirementId" to snapshot.requirementInternalId,
   637→                        "changeType" to snapshot.changeType.name,
   638→                        "shortreq" to snapshot.shortreq,
   639→                        "previousShortreq" to snapshot.previousShortreq,
   640→                        "details" to snapshot.details,
   641→                        "previousDetails" to snapshot.previousDetails,
   642→                        "chapter" to snapshot.chapter,
   643→                        "previousChapter" to snapshot.previousChapter,
   644→                        "changeSummary" to snapshot.getChangeSummary(),
   645→                        "existingReview" to if (existingReview != null) mapOf(
   646→                            "id" to existingReview.id,
   647→                            "assessment" to existingReview.assessment.name,
   648→                            "comment" to existingReview.comment
   649→                        ) else null
   650→                    )
   651→                },
   652→                "isOpen" to session.isOpen()
   653→            ))
   654→        } catch (e: NoSuchElementException) {
   655→            return HttpResponse.notFound(mapOf(
   656→                "error" to "Not Found",
   657→                "message" to "Invalid or expired review token"
   658→            ))
   659→        }
   660→    }
   661→
   662→    /**
   663→     * POST /api/alignment/review/{token}/complete - Mark review as complete
   664→     */
   665→    @Post("/alignment/review/{token}/complete")
   666→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   667→    fun completeReviewByToken(@PathVariable token: String): HttpResponse<Map<String, Any>> {
   668→        try {
   669→            val reviewer = alignmentService.getReviewerByToken(token)
   670→            val completedReviewer = alignmentService.completeReview(reviewer.id!!)
   671→
   672→            return HttpResponse.ok(mapOf(
   673→                "success" to true,
   674→                "message" to "Review marked as complete",
   675→                "status" to completedReviewer.status.name,
   676→                "reviewedCount" to completedReviewer.reviewedCount
   677→            ))
   678→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   679→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   680→                "error" to "Bad Request",
   681→                "message" to (e.message ?: "Cannot complete review")
   682→            ))
   683→        } catch (e: NoSuchElementException) {
   684→            return HttpResponse.notFound(mapOf(
   685→                "error" to "Not Found",
   686→                "message" to "Invalid or expired review token"
   687→            ))
   688→        }
   689→    }
   690→
   691→    // ========== Review Export/Import Endpoints ==========
   692→
   693→    /**
   694→     * GET /api/alignment/review/{token}/export - Export review as Excel
   695→     * Returns .xlsx with assessment dropdown validation for offline review.
   696→     */
   697→    @Get("/alignment/review/{token}/export")
   698→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   699→    fun exportReviewToExcel(@PathVariable token: String): HttpResponse<*> {
   700→        try {
   701→            val reviewer = alignmentService.getReviewerByToken(token)
   702→            val session = reviewer.session
   703→            val snapshots = alignmentService.getSnapshotsForSession(session.id!!)
   704→            val existingReviews = alignmentService.getReviewsByReviewer(reviewer.id!!)
   705→                .associateBy { it.snapshot.id }
   706→
   707→            val workbook = createReviewExcelWorkbook(snapshots, existingReviews)
   708→            val outputStream = ByteArrayOutputStream()
   709→            workbook.write(outputStream)
   710→            workbook.close()
   711→
   712→            val inputStream = ByteArrayInputStream(outputStream.toByteArray())
   713→            val filename = "review_${session.release.name}_v${session.release.version}.xlsx"
   714→                .replace(" ", "_")
   715→
   716→            return HttpResponse.ok(StreamedFile(inputStream, MediaType.of("application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet")))
   717→                .header("Content-Disposition", "attachment; filename=\"$filename\"")
   718→        } catch (e: NoSuchElementException) {
   719→            return HttpResponse.notFound(mapOf(
   720→                "error" to "Not Found",
   721→                "message" to "Invalid or expired review token"
   722→            ))
   723→        }
   724→    }
   725→
   726→    /**
   727→     * POST /api/alignment/review/{token}/import - Import reviews from Excel
   728→     * Parses .xlsx and submits reviews for each row with a non-empty Assessment.
   729→     *
   730→     * Validation layers:
   731→     * 1. File-level: size, extension, content-type
   732→     * 2. Structure: "Review" sheet required, all expected headers present
   733→     * 3. Row-level: snapshot ID cross-reference, assessment enum, comment length, duplicate detection
   734→     * 4. Limits: max 1000 data rows, max 50 reported errors
   735→     */
   736→    @Post("/alignment/review/{token}/import", consumes = [MediaType.MULTIPART_FORM_DATA])
   737→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_ANONYMOUS)
   738→    fun importReviewsFromExcel(
   739→        @PathVariable token: String,
   740→        @Part file: CompletedFileUpload
   741→    ): HttpResponse<Map<String, Any>> {
   742→        logger.info("Importing reviews from Excel for token")
   743→
   744→        try {
   745→            // --- File-level validation ---
   746→            val originalFilename = file.filename
   747→            if (file.size > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
   748→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   749→                    "success" to false,
   750→                    "message" to "File size exceeds 10MB limit"
   751→                ))
   752→            }
   753→            if (!originalFilename.endsWith(".xlsx", ignoreCase = true)) {
   754→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   755→                    "success" to false,
   756→                    "message" to "Only .xlsx files are supported"
   757→                ))
   758→            }
   759→            val contentType = file.contentType.map { it.toString() }.orElse("")
   760→            if (contentType.isNotEmpty()
   761→                && !contentType.contains("spreadsheetml.sheet", ignoreCase = true)
   762→                && !contentType.contains("excel", ignoreCase = true)
   763→                && !contentType.contains("octet-stream", ignoreCase = true)) {
   764→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   765→                    "success" to false,
   766→                    "message" to "Invalid file type. Please upload a valid .xlsx Excel file"
   767→                ))
   768→            }
   769→
   770→            val reviewer = alignmentService.getReviewerByToken(token)
   771→            val session = reviewer.session
   772→
   773→            if (!session.isOpen()) {
   774→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   775→                    "success" to false,
   776→                    "message" to "Review session is closed"
   777→                ))
   778→            }
   779→
   780→            // Build snapshot lookup: ID -> requirementInternalId (for cross-reference)
   781→            val snapshots = alignmentService.getSnapshotsForSession(session.id!!)
   782→            val snapshotById = snapshots.associateBy { it.id }
   783→            val validSnapshotIds = snapshotById.keys.filterNotNull().toSet()
   784→
   785→            val workbook = XSSFWorkbook(file.inputStream)
   786→
   787→            // --- Structural validation: require "Review" sheet ---
   788→            val sheet = workbook.getSheet("Review")
   789→            if (sheet == null) {
   790→                workbook.close()
   791→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   792→                    "success" to false,
   793→                    "message" to "Excel file must contain a sheet named 'Review'. " +
   794→                        "Please use the exported file as a template."
   795→                ))
   796→            }
   797→
   798→            val headerRow = sheet.getRow(0)
   799→            if (headerRow == null) {
   800→                workbook.close()
   801→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   802→                    "success" to false,
   803→                    "message" to "Excel file has no header row"
   804→                ))
   805→            }
   806→
   807→            // Map headers case-insensitively
   808→            val headerMap = mutableMapOf<String, Int>()
   809→            for (i in 0 until headerRow.lastCellNum) {
   810→                val cell = headerRow.getCell(i) ?: continue
   811→                val cellValue = try { cell.stringCellValue.trim().lowercase() } catch (e: Exception) { continue }
   812→                headerMap[cellValue] = i
   813→            }
   814→
   815→            // Validate ALL expected headers are present
   816→            val expectedHeaders = listOf(
   817→                "snapshot id", "requirement id", "change type", "chapter",
   818→                "short req", "details", "previous short req", "previous details",
   819→                "assessment", "comment"
   820→            )
   821→            val missingHeaders = expectedHeaders.filter { it !in headerMap }
   822→            if (missingHeaders.isNotEmpty()) {
   823→                workbook.close()
   824→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   825→                    "success" to false,
   826→                    "message" to "Missing required columns: ${missingHeaders.joinToString(", ") { "'${it}'" }}. " +
   827→                        "Please use the exported file as a template without modifying the header row."
   828→                ))
   829→            }
   830→
   831→            val snapshotIdCol = headerMap["snapshot id"]!!
   832→            val requirementIdCol = headerMap["requirement id"]!!
   833→            val assessmentCol = headerMap["assessment"]!!
   834→            val commentCol = headerMap["comment"]!!
   835→
   836→            // --- Row count limit ---
   837→            val maxRows = 1000
   838→            val dataRowCount = sheet.lastRowNum // row 0 is header
   839→            if (dataRowCount > maxRows) {
   840→                workbook.close()
   841→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   842→                    "success" to false,
   843→                    "message" to "File contains $dataRowCount data rows, exceeding the limit of $maxRows"
   844→                ))
   845→            }
   846→
   847→            var imported = 0
   848→            var skipped = 0
   849→            val errors = mutableListOf<String>()
   850→            val maxErrors = 50
   851→            val seenSnapshotIds = mutableSetOf<Long>()
   852→            val maxCommentLength = 2000
   853→
   854→            for (rowNum in 1..sheet.lastRowNum) {
   855→                val row = sheet.getRow(rowNum) ?: continue
   856→
   857→                // Read assessment value safely (handle non-string cells)
   858→                val assessmentValue = try {
   859→                    val cell = row.getCell(assessmentCol)
   860→                    cell?.stringCellValue?.trim() ?: ""
   861→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   862→                    try {
   863→                        row.getCell(assessmentCol)?.toString()?.trim() ?: ""
   864→                    } catch (e2: Exception) { "" }
   865→                }
   866→                if (assessmentValue.isEmpty()) {
   867→                    skipped++
   868→                    continue
   869→                }
   870→
   871→                // Stop collecting error details after limit (still process rows)
   872→                val canAddError = errors.size < maxErrors
   873→
   874→                // Parse Snapshot ID (numeric or string)
   875→                val snapshotId = try {
   876→                    row.getCell(snapshotIdCol)?.numericCellValue?.toLong()
   877→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   878→                    try {
   879→                        row.getCell(snapshotIdCol)?.stringCellValue?.trim()?.toLongOrNull()
   880→                    } catch (e2: Exception) { null }
   881→                }
   882→
   883→                if (snapshotId == null) {
   884→                    if (canAddError) errors.add("Row ${rowNum + 1}: Invalid or missing Snapshot ID")
   885→                    continue
   886→                }
   887→
   888→                // Validate snapshot belongs to session
   889→                if (snapshotId !in validSnapshotIds) {
   890→                    if (canAddError) errors.add("Row ${rowNum + 1}: Snapshot ID $snapshotId does not belong to this review session")
   891→                    continue
   892→                }
   893→
   894→                // Cross-reference: verify Requirement ID matches the snapshot
   895→                val expectedReqId = snapshotById[snapshotId]?.requirementInternalId
   896→                val rowReqId = try {
   897→                    row.getCell(requirementIdCol)?.stringCellValue?.trim()
   898→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   899→                    try { row.getCell(requirementIdCol)?.toString()?.trim() } catch (e2: Exception) { null }
   900→                }
   901→                if (rowReqId != null && rowReqId.isNotEmpty() && rowReqId != expectedReqId) {
   902→                    if (canAddError) errors.add("Row ${rowNum + 1}: Requirement ID '$rowReqId' does not match Snapshot ID $snapshotId (expected '$expectedReqId'). Row data may have been modified.")
   903→                    continue
   904→                }
   905→
   906→                // Duplicate detection within file
   907→                if (snapshotId in seenSnapshotIds) {
   908→                    if (canAddError) errors.add("Row ${rowNum + 1}: Duplicate Snapshot ID $snapshotId (already processed earlier in file)")
   909→                    continue
   910→                }
   911→                seenSnapshotIds.add(snapshotId)
   912→
   913→                // Validate assessment enum strictly
   914→                val assessment = try {
   915→                    ReviewAssessment.valueOf(assessmentValue.uppercase())
   916→                } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   917→                    if (canAddError) errors.add("Row ${rowNum + 1}: Invalid assessment '$assessmentValue' (must be OK, CHANGE, or NOGO)")
   918→                    continue
   919→                }
   920→
   921→                // Read and validate comment length
   922→                val rawComment = try {
   923→                    row.getCell(commentCol)?.stringCellValue?.trim()
   924→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   925→                    try { row.getCell(commentCol)?.toString()?.trim() } catch (e2: Exception) { null }
   926→                }
   927→                val comment = if (rawComment.isNullOrEmpty()) null else rawComment
   928→                if (comment != null && comment.length > maxCommentLength) {
   929→                    if (canAddError) errors.add("Row ${rowNum + 1}: Comment exceeds $maxCommentLength character limit (${comment.length} chars)")
   930→                    continue
   931→                }
   932→
   933→                try {
   934→                    alignmentService.submitReviewByToken(token, snapshotId, assessment, comment)
   935→                    imported++
   936→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   937→                    if (canAddError) errors.add("Row ${rowNum + 1}: ${e.message}")
   938→                }
   939→            }
   940→
   941→            workbook.close()
   942→
   943→            if (errors.size >= maxErrors) {
   944→                errors.add("... (showing first $maxErrors errors only)")
   945→            }
   946→
   947→            return HttpResponse.ok(mapOf(
   948→                "success" to true,
   949→                "imported" to imported,
   950→                "skipped" to skipped,
   951→                "errors" to errors,
   952→                "message" to "Imported $imported review(s), skipped $skipped, ${errors.size} error(s)"
   953→            ))
   954→        } catch (e: NoSuchElementException) {
   955→            return HttpResponse.notFound(mapOf(
   956→                "success" to false,
   957→                "message" to "Invalid or expired review token"
   958→            ))
   959→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   960→            logger.error("Failed to import reviews from Excel", e)
   961→            return HttpResponse.badRequest(mapOf(
   962→                "success" to false,
   963→                "message" to "Failed to parse Excel file: ${e.message}"
   964→            ))
   965→        }
   966→    }
   967→
   968→    // ========== Helper Methods ==========
   969→
   970→    private fun toSessionResponse(session: com.secman.domain.AlignmentSession): Map<String, Any?> {
   971→        return mapOf(
   972→            "id" to session.id,
   973→            "releaseId" to session.release.id,
   974→            "releaseName" to session.release.name,
   975→            "releaseVersion" to session.release.version,
   976→            "status" to session.status.name,
   977→            "changedRequirementsCount" to session.changedRequirementsCount,
   978→            "initiatedBy" to session.initiatedBy.username,
   979→            "baselineReleaseId" to session.baselineRelease?.id,
   980→            "reviewScope" to session.reviewScope.name,
   981→            "startedAt" to session.startedAt?.toString(),
   982→            "completedAt" to session.completedAt?.toString(),
   983→            "completionNotes" to session.completionNotes
   984→        )
   985→    }
   986→
   987→    private fun createReviewExcelWorkbook(
   988→        snapshots: List<com.secman.domain.AlignmentSnapshot>,
   989→        existingReviews: Map<Long?, com.secman.domain.RequirementReview>
   990→    ): XSSFWorkbook {
   991→        val workbook = XSSFWorkbook()
   992→        val sheet = workbook.createSheet("Review")
   993→
   994→        val headers = arrayOf(
   995→            "Snapshot ID", "Requirement ID", "Change Type", "Chapter",
   996→            "Short Req", "Details", "Previous Short Req", "Previous Details",
   997→            "Assessment", "Comment"
   998→        )
   999→
  1000→        // Header style
  1001→        val headerStyle = workbook.createCellStyle()
  1002→        val headerFont = workbook.createFont()
  1003→        headerFont.bold = true
  1004→        headerStyle.setFont(headerFont)
  1005→
  1006→        val headerRow = sheet.createRow(0)
  1007→        headers.forEachIndexed { index, header ->
  1008→            val cell = headerRow.createCell(index)
  1009→            cell.setCellValue(header)
  1010→            cell.cellStyle = headerStyle
  1011→        }
  1012→
  1013→        // Data rows
  1014→        snapshots.forEachIndexed { index, snapshot ->
  1015→            val row = sheet.createRow(index + 1)
  1016→            row.createCell(0).setCellValue(snapshot.id?.toDouble() ?: 0.0)
  1017→            row.createCell(1).setCellValue(snapshot.requirementInternalId)
  1018→            row.createCell(2).setCellValue(snapshot.changeType.name)
  1019→            row.createCell(3).setCellValue(snapshot.chapter ?: "")
  1020→            row.createCell(4).setCellValue(snapshot.shortreq)
  1021→            row.createCell(5).setCellValue(snapshot.details ?: "")
  1022→            row.createCell(6).setCellValue(snapshot.previousShortreq ?: "")
  1023→            row.createCell(7).setCellValue(snapshot.previousDetails ?: "")
  1024→
  1025→            // Pre-populate from existing reviews
  1026→            val review = existingReviews[snapshot.id]
  1027→            row.createCell(8).setCellValue(review?.assessment?.name ?: "")
  1028→            row.createCell(9).setCellValue(review?.comment ?: "")
  1029→        }
  1030→
  1031→        // Add data validation dropdown for Assessment column (column 8)
  1032→        if (snapshots.isNotEmpty()) {
  1033→            val validationHelper = sheet.dataValidationHelper
  1034→            val constraint = validationHelper.createExplicitListConstraint(arrayOf("OK", "CHANGE", "NOGO"))
  1035→            val addressList = CellRangeAddressList(1, snapshots.size, 8, 8)
  1036→            val validation = validationHelper.createValidation(constraint, addressList)
  1037→            validation.showErrorBox = true
  1038→            validation.createErrorBox("Invalid Assessment", "Please select OK, CHANGE, or NOGO")
  1039→            sheet.addValidationData(validation)
  1040→        }
  1041→
  1042→        // Auto-size columns
  1043→        for (i in headers.indices) {
  1044→            sheet.autoSizeColumn(i)
  1045→            if (sheet.getColumnWidth(i) < 3000) {
  1046→                sheet.setColumnWidth(i, 3000)
  1047→            }
  1048→        }
  1049→
  1050→        return workbook
  1051→    }
  1052→
  1053→    private fun toAlignmentStatusResponse(status: AlignmentService.AlignmentStatusResult): Map<String, Any?> {
  1054→        return mapOf(
  1055→            "session" to toSessionResponse(status.session),
  1056→            "reviewers" to mapOf(
  1057→                "total" to status.totalReviewers,
  1058→                "completed" to status.completedReviewers,
  1059→                "inProgress" to status.inProgressReviewers,
  1060→                "pending" to status.pendingReviewers,
  1061→                "completionPercent" to if (status.totalReviewers > 0) {
  1062→                    (status.completedReviewers * 100 / status.totalReviewers)
  1063→                } else 0
  1064→            ),
  1065→            "requirements" to mapOf(
  1066→                "total" to status.totalRequirements,
  1067→                "reviewed" to status.reviewedRequirements
  1068→            ),
  1069→            "assessments" to mapOf(
  1070→                "ok" to status.assessmentSummary.okCount,
  1071→                "change" to status.assessmentSummary.changeCount,
  1072→                "nogo" to status.assessmentSummary.nogoCount
  1073→            )
  1074→        )
  1075→    }
  1076→}
  1077→
  1078→// ========== Request DTOs ==========
  1079→
  1080→@Serdeable
  1081→data class StartAlignmentRequest(
  1082→    val reviewAll: Boolean = false
  1083→)
  1084→
  1085→@Serdeable
  1086→data class SubmitReviewRequest(
  1087→    val snapshotId: Long,
  1088→    val assessment: ReviewAssessment,
  1089→    val comment: String? = null
  1090→)
  1091→
  1092→@Serdeable
  1093→data class FinalizeAlignmentRequest(
  1094→    val activateRelease: Boolean = true,
  1095→    val notes: String? = null
  1096→)
  1097→
  1098→@Serdeable
  1099→data class CancelAlignmentRequest(
  1100→    val notes: String? = null
  1101→)
  1102→
  1103→@Serdeable
  1104→data class SubmitReviewDecisionRequest(
  1105→    val reviewId: Long,
  1106→    val decision: Decision,
  1107→    val comment: String? = null
  1108→)
  1109→

data.prompt:
Explore the alignment/review system in this Kotlin/Micronaut codebase. I need to understand:

1. The domain model for alignment sessions, reviews, and reviewer feedback (look in src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/domain/)
2. The AlignmentService and AlignmentController - what endpoints exist, how review data is stored and queried
3. The AlignmentSession entity - fields, relationships, especially how reviewer responses (OK, CHANGE, NOGO) and comments are stored
4. Any existing export functionality in the alignment system

Search thoroughly in:
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/domain/ for alignment-related entities
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/service/AlignmentService.kt
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/controller/AlignmentController.kt
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/repository/ for alignment repositories

Be very thorough - read the full files, not just snippets.

Explore the frontend alignment dashboard components. I need to understand:

1. The alignment dashboard page/component that shows reviews for a release
2. How the frontend fetches alignment/review data from the backend
3. The AlignmentResultsPage component (it's an untracked new file)
4. Any existing download/export buttons in the alignment UI
5. The release detail page that links to the alignment dashboard

Search in:
- src/frontend/src/components/ for alignment-related components
- src/frontend/src/pages/alignment/ for alignment pages
- src/frontend/src/services/ for alignment-related API services
- Look for files matching *lignment* or *review*

Be thorough - read the full component files to understand the UI structure and data flow.

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.Asset
     4→import com.secman.dto.AssetExportDto
     5→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.Authentication
     6→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
     7→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.CellStyle
     8→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.FillPatternType
     9→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.IndexedColors
    10→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Workbook
    11→import org.apache.poi.xssf.streaming.SXSSFWorkbook
    12→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    13→import java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream
    14→import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter
    15→
    16→/**
    17→ * Service for exporting assets to Excel format
    18→ * Feature: 029-asset-bulk-operations (User Story 2 - Export Assets to File)
    19→ *
    20→ * Related Requirements:
    21→ * - FR-010: Export assets to Excel with all fields
    22→ * - FR-011: Apply workgroup-based access control to exports
    23→ * - FR-012: Format export file with clear column headers
    24→ * - FR-013: Include workgroup names in readable format
    25→ * - FR-015: Handle empty asset list with error message
    26→ *
    27→ * Performance Target:
    28→ * - Export 10K assets in <15 seconds (SC-002)
    29→ *
    30→ * Technical Approach:
    31→ * - Use SXSSFWorkbook(100) for streaming export (constant memory)
    32→ * - Create CellStyle objects ONCE before loop (not per cell)
    33→ * - Use fixed column widths (auto-sizing adds 3 minutes for 10K rows)
    34→ * - Dispose workbook in finally block to clean up temp files
    35→ */
    36→@Singleton
    37→class AssetExportService(
    38→    private val assetFilterService: AssetFilterService
    39→) {
    40→
    41→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AssetExportService::class.java)
    42→
    43→    // Date formatter for Excel cells
    44→    private val dateFormatter = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
    45→
    46→    /**
    47→     * Export assets accessible to the authenticated user
    48→     * FR-011: Workgroup-based access control (ADMIN sees all, non-ADMIN sees workgroup+owned)
    49→     *
    50→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
    51→     * @return List of AssetExportDto ready for Excel serialization
    52→     */
    53→    fun exportAssets(authentication: Authentication): List<AssetExportDto> {
    54→        log.info("Exporting assets for user: {}", authentication.name)
    55→
    56→        // Get assets with workgroup filtering
    57→        val assets: List<Asset> = assetFilterService.getAccessibleAssets(authentication)
    58→
    59→        log.info("Found {} assets accessible to user {}", assets.size, authentication.name)
    60→
    61→        // Convert to export DTOs
    62→        return assets.map { asset -> AssetExportDto.fromAsset(asset) }
    63→    }
    64→
    65→    /**
    66→     * Write asset export DTOs to Excel workbook
    67→     * FR-012, FR-013: Format with clear headers and fixed column widths
    68→     *
    69→     * Performance optimizations from research.md:
    70→     * - SXSSFWorkbook with 100-row memory window
    71→     * - Create styles ONCE before loop
    72→     * - Fixed column widths (not auto-sizing)
    73→     * - Enable GZIP compression for temp files
    74→     *
    75→     * @param dtos List of asset export DTOs
    76→     * @return ByteArrayOutputStream containing Excel workbook
    77→     */
    78→    fun writeToExcel(dtos: List<AssetExportDto>): ByteArrayOutputStream {
    79→        log.info("Writing {} assets to Excel format", dtos.size)
    80→
    81→        // Create streaming workbook with 100-row window
    82→        val workbook = SXSSFWorkbook(100)
    83→        workbook.setCompressTempFiles(true)
    84→
    85→        try {
    86→            val sheet = workbook.createSheet("Assets")
    87→
    88→            // Create styles ONCE (not per cell) - critical for performance
    89→            val styles = createAssetStyles(workbook)
    90→
    91→            // Write header row
    92→            createHeaderRow(sheet, styles.header, workbook)
    93→
    94→            // Write data rows
    95→            dtos.forEachIndexed { index, dto ->
    96→                createAssetRow(sheet, index + 1, dto, styles)
    97→            }
    98→
    99→            // Set fixed column widths (auto-sizing adds 3 minutes for 10K rows)
   100→            setFixedColumnWidths(sheet)
   101→
   102→            // Write to output stream
   103→            val outputStream = ByteArrayOutputStream()
   104→            workbook.write(outputStream)
   105→
   106→            log.info("Excel export complete: {} rows written", dtos.size)
   107→
   108→            return outputStream
   109→
   110→        } finally {
   111→            // CRITICAL: Close workbook to clean up temp files
   112→            workbook.close()
   113→            log.debug("SXSSFWorkbook closed, temp files cleaned")
   114→        }
   115→    }
   116→
   117→    /**
   118→     * Create cell styles for Excel export (created ONCE, reused for all cells)
   119→     */
   120→    private fun createAssetStyles(workbook: Workbook): AssetStyles {
   121→        // Header style
   122→        val headerStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   123→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.GREY_25_PERCENT.index
   124→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   125→            val font = workbook.createFont()
   126→            font.bold = true
   127→            setFont(font)
   128→        }
   129→
   130→        // Date style
   131→        val dateStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   132→            dataFormat = workbook.creationHelper.createDataFormat().getFormat("yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss")
   133→        }
   134→
   135→        // Text style (default)
   136→        val textStyle = workbook.createCellStyle()
   137→
   138→        // Wrap text style for descriptions
   139→        val wrapTextStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   140→            wrapText = true
   141→        }
   142→
   143→        return AssetStyles(headerStyle, dateStyle, textStyle, wrapTextStyle)
   144→    }
   145→
   146→    /**
   147→     * Create header row with column names
   148→     */
   149→    private fun createHeaderRow(sheet: org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Sheet, headerStyle: CellStyle, workbook: Workbook) {
   150→        val headerRow = sheet.createRow(0)
   151→        val headers = listOf(
   152→            "Name", "Type", "IP Address", "Owner", "Description",
   153→            "Groups", "Cloud Account ID", "Cloud Instance ID", "OS Version", "AD Domain",
   154→            "Workgroups", "Created At", "Updated At", "Last Seen"
   155→        )
   156→
   157→        headers.forEachIndexed { index, header ->
   158→            val cell = headerRow.createCell(index)
   159→            cell.setCellValue(header)
   160→            cell.cellStyle = headerStyle
   161→        }
   162→    }
   163→
   164→    /**
   165→     * Create data row for single asset
   166→     */
   167→    private fun createAssetRow(
   168→        sheet: org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Sheet,
   169→        rowNum: Int,
   170→        dto: AssetExportDto,
   171→        styles: AssetStyles
   172→    ) {
   173→        val row = sheet.createRow(rowNum)
   174→
   175→        // Name
   176→        row.createCell(0).apply {
   177→            setCellValue(dto.name)
   178→            cellStyle = styles.text
   179→        }
   180→
   181→        // Type
   182→        row.createCell(1).apply {
   183→            setCellValue(dto.type)
   184→            cellStyle = styles.text
   185→        }
   186→
   187→        // IP Address
   188→        row.createCell(2).apply {
   189→            setCellValue(dto.ip ?: "")
   190→            cellStyle = styles.text
   191→        }
   192→
   193→        // Owner
   194→        row.createCell(3).apply {
   195→            setCellValue(dto.owner)
   196→            cellStyle = styles.text
   197→        }
   198→
   199→        // Description
   200→        row.createCell(4).apply {
   201→            setCellValue(dto.description ?: "")
   202→            cellStyle = styles.wrapText
   203→        }
   204→
   205→        // Groups
   206→        row.createCell(5).apply {
   207→            setCellValue(dto.groups ?: "")
   208→            cellStyle = styles.text
   209→        }
   210→
   211→        // Cloud Account ID
   212→        row.createCell(6).apply {
   213→            setCellValue(dto.cloudAccountId ?: "")
   214→            cellStyle = styles.text
   215→        }
   216→
   217→        // Cloud Instance ID
   218→        row.createCell(7).apply {
   219→            setCellValue(dto.cloudInstanceId ?: "")
   220→            cellStyle = styles.text
   221→        }
   222→
   223→        // OS Version
   224→        row.createCell(8).apply {
   225→            setCellValue(dto.osVersion ?: "")
   226→            cellStyle = styles.text
   227→        }
   228→
   229→        // AD Domain
   230→        row.createCell(9).apply {
   231→            setCellValue(dto.adDomain ?: "")
   232→            cellStyle = styles.text
   233→        }
   234→
   235→        // Workgroups (comma-separated)
   236→        row.createCell(10).apply {
   237→            setCellValue(dto.workgroups)
   238→            cellStyle = styles.text
   239→        }
   240→
   241→        // Created At
   242→        row.createCell(11).apply {
   243→            setCellValue(dto.createdAt?.format(dateFormatter) ?: "")
   244→            cellStyle = styles.text
   245→        }
   246→
   247→        // Updated At
   248→        row.createCell(12).apply {
   249→            setCellValue(dto.updatedAt?.format(dateFormatter) ?: "")
   250→            cellStyle = styles.text
   251→        }
   252→
   253→        // Last Seen
   254→        row.createCell(13).apply {
   255→            setCellValue(dto.lastSeen?.format(dateFormatter) ?: "")
   256→            cellStyle = styles.text
   257→        }
   258→    }
   259→
   260→    /**
   261→     * Set fixed column widths (auto-sizing adds 3 minutes overhead for 10K rows)
   262→     */
   263→    private fun setFixedColumnWidths(sheet: org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Sheet) {
   264→        sheet.setColumnWidth(0, 6000)  // Name
   265→        sheet.setColumnWidth(1, 4000)  // Type
   266→        sheet.setColumnWidth(2, 4000)  // IP Address
   267→        sheet.setColumnWidth(3, 5000)  // Owner
   268→        sheet.setColumnWidth(4, 8000)  // Description
   269→        sheet.setColumnWidth(5, 5000)  // Groups
   270→        sheet.setColumnWidth(6, 5000)  // Cloud Account ID
   271→        sheet.setColumnWidth(7, 5000)  // Cloud Instance ID
   272→        sheet.setColumnWidth(8, 4000)  // OS Version
   273→        sheet.setColumnWidth(9, 5000)  // AD Domain
   274→        sheet.setColumnWidth(10, 6000) // Workgroups
   275→        sheet.setColumnWidth(11, 5000) // Created At
   276→        sheet.setColumnWidth(12, 5000) // Updated At
   277→        sheet.setColumnWidth(13, 5000) // Last Seen
   278→    }
   279→
   280→    /**
   281→     * Data class for style objects (created once, reused)
   282→     */
   283→    private data class AssetStyles(
   284→        val header: CellStyle,
   285→        val date: CellStyle,
   286→        val text: CellStyle,
   287→        val wrapText: CellStyle
   288→    )
   289→}
   290→

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.ExceptionRequestStatus
     4→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityExceptionRequestRepository
     5→import io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable
     6→import jakarta.inject.Inject
     7→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
     8→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.*
     9→import org.apache.poi.xssf.streaming.SXSSFWorkbook
    10→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    11→import java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream
    12→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    13→import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter
    14→
    15→/**
    16→ * Service for exporting vulnerability exception requests to Excel.
    17→ *
    18→ * **Export Features**:
    19→ * - Streams large datasets using SXSSFWorkbook (100-row memory window)
    20→ * - Filters by status, date range, requester, reviewer
    21→ * - Professional formatting with headers and styles
    22→ * - Fixed column widths for consistent appearance
    23→ *
    24→ * **Excel Format**:
    25→ * - Request ID, CVE ID, Asset Name, Asset IP, Requester, Submission Date
    26→ * - Status, Reviewer, Review Date, Reason, Review Comment, Expiration Date, Auto-Approved
    27→ *
    28→ * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
    29→ * User Story 8: Analytics & Reporting (P3)
    30→ * Phase 11: Analytics & Reporting
    31→ * Reference: spec.md FR-026, acceptance scenario US8-3
    32→ */
    33→@Singleton
    34→open class ExceptionRequestExportService(
    35→    @Inject private val requestRepository: VulnerabilityExceptionRequestRepository
    36→) {
    37→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ExceptionRequestExportService::class.java)
    38→
    39→    companion object {
    40→        private val DATE_FORMATTER = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
    41→        private val DATE_ONLY_FORMATTER = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy-MM-dd")
    42→    }
    43→
    44→    /**
    45→     * Export exception requests to Excel with optional filtering.
    46→     *
    47→     * @param status Optional status filter
    48→     * @param dateRange Optional date range (7days, 30days, 90days, alltime)
    49→     * @param requesterId Optional requester user ID filter
    50→     * @param reviewerId Optional reviewer user ID filter
    51→     * @return ByteArrayOutputStream containing Excel workbook
    52→     */
    53→    open fun exportToExcel(
    54→        status: ExceptionRequestStatus? = null,
    55→        dateRange: String? = null,
    56→        requesterId: Long? = null,
    57→        reviewerId: Long? = null
    58→    ): ByteArrayOutputStream {
    59→        logger.info("Exporting exception requests: status={}, dateRange={}, requesterId={}, reviewerId={}",
    60→            status, dateRange, requesterId, reviewerId)
    61→
    62→        // Get filtered requests
    63→        val requests = getFilteredRequests(status, dateRange, requesterId, reviewerId)
    64→
    65→        logger.debug("Exporting {} exception requests to Excel", requests.size)
    66→
    67→        // Create streaming workbook with 100-row window
    68→        val workbook = SXSSFWorkbook(100)
    69→        workbook.setCompressTempFiles(true)
    70→
    71→        try {
    72→            val sheet = workbook.createSheet("Exception Requests")
    73→
    74→            // Create styles once and reuse (prevent 64K style limit)
    75→            val styles = createStyles(workbook)
    76→
    77→            // Create header row
    78→            createHeaderRow(sheet, styles.header)
    79→
    80→            // Create data rows
    81→            var rowNum = 1
    82→            for (request in requests) {
    83→                createDataRow(sheet, rowNum++, request, styles.normal)
    84→            }
    85→
    86→            // Set column widths (fixed widths for performance)
    87→            setColumnWidths(sheet)
    88→
    89→            // Write to output stream
    90→            val outputStream = ByteArrayOutputStream()
    91→            workbook.write(outputStream)
    92→
    93→            logger.info("Excel export completed: {} rows written", requests.size)
    94→
    95→            return outputStream
    96→
    97→        } finally {
    98→            // CRITICAL: Close workbook to clean up temp files
    99→            workbook.close()
   100→            logger.debug("SXSSFWorkbook closed, temp files cleaned")
   101→        }
   102→    }
   103→
   104→    /**
   105→     * Get filtered exception requests based on parameters.
   106→     */
   107→    private fun getFilteredRequests(
   108→        status: ExceptionRequestStatus?,
   109→        dateRange: String?,
   110→        requesterId: Long?,
   111→        reviewerId: Long?
   112→    ): List<com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityExceptionRequest> {
   113→        // Calculate date range start
   114→        val since = when (dateRange?.lowercase()) {
   115→            "7days" -> LocalDateTime.now().minusDays(7)
   116→            "30days" -> LocalDateTime.now().minusDays(30)
   117→            "90days" -> LocalDateTime.now().minusDays(90)
   118→            "alltime", null -> null
   119→            else -> LocalDateTime.now().minusDays(30)
   120→        }
   121→
   122→        // Build filter query
   123→        return when {
   124→            // Status and date filter
   125→            status != null && since != null -> {
   126→                requestRepository.findByStatusAndCreatedAtAfter(status, since)
   127→            }
   128→            // Status only
   129→            status != null -> {
   130→                requestRepository.findByStatus(status, Pageable.UNPAGED).content
   131→            }
   132→            // Date only
   133→            since != null -> {
   134→                requestRepository.findByCreatedAtAfter(since)
   135→            }
   136→            // No filters - all requests
   137→            else -> {
   138→                requestRepository.findAll().toList()
   139→            }
   140→        }.filter { request ->
   141→            // Apply additional filters (requester, reviewer)
   142→            val matchesRequester = requesterId == null || request.requestedByUser?.id == requesterId
   143→            val matchesReviewer = reviewerId == null || request.reviewedByUser?.id == reviewerId
   144→            matchesRequester && matchesReviewer
   145→        }
   146→    }
   147→
   148→    /**
   149→     * Create header row with column titles.
   150→     */
   151→    private fun createHeaderRow(sheet: Sheet, headerStyle: CellStyle) {
   152→        val headerRow = sheet.createRow(0)
   153→
   154→        val headers = listOf(
   155→            "Request ID",
   156→            "CVE ID",
   157→            "Asset Name",
   158→            "Asset IP",
   159→            "Requester",
   160→            "Submission Date",
   161→            "Status",
   162→            "Reviewer",
   163→            "Review Date",
   164→            "Reason",
   165→            "Review Comment",
   166→            "Expiration Date",
   167→            "Auto-Approved"
   168→        )
   169→
   170→        headers.forEachIndexed { index, title ->
   171→            val cell = headerRow.createCell(index)
   172→            cell.setCellValue(title)
   173→            cell.cellStyle = headerStyle
   174→        }
   175→    }
   176→
   177→    /**
   178→     * Create data row for an exception request.
   179→     */
   180→    private fun createDataRow(
   181→        sheet: Sheet,
   182→        rowNum: Int,
   183→        request: com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityExceptionRequest,
   184→        normalStyle: CellStyle
   185→    ) {
   186→        val row = sheet.createRow(rowNum)
   187→
   188→        var colNum = 0
   189→
   190→        // Request ID
   191→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   192→            setCellValue(request.id?.toDouble() ?: 0.0)
   193→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   194→        }
   195→
   196→        // CVE ID
   197→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   198→            setCellValue(request.vulnerability?.vulnerabilityId ?: "N/A")
   199→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   200→        }
   201→
   202→        // Asset Name
   203→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   204→            setCellValue(request.vulnerability?.asset?.name ?: "N/A")
   205→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   206→        }
   207→
   208→        // Asset IP
   209→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   210→            setCellValue(request.vulnerability?.asset?.ip ?: "N/A")
   211→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   212→        }
   213→
   214→        // Requester
   215→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   216→            setCellValue(request.requestedByUsername)
   217→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   218→        }
   219→
   220→        // Submission Date
   221→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   222→            setCellValue(request.createdAt?.format(DATE_FORMATTER) ?: "N/A")
   223→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   224→        }
   225→
   226→        // Status
   227→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   228→            setCellValue(request.status.name)
   229→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   230→        }
   231→
   232→        // Reviewer
   233→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   234→            setCellValue(request.reviewedByUsername ?: "N/A")
   235→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   236→        }
   237→
   238→        // Review Date
   239→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   240→            setCellValue(request.reviewDate?.format(DATE_FORMATTER) ?: "N/A")
   241→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   242→        }
   243→
   244→        // Reason
   245→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   246→            setCellValue(request.reason)
   247→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   248→        }
   249→
   250→        // Review Comment
   251→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   252→            setCellValue(request.reviewComment ?: "N/A")
   253→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   254→        }
   255→
   256→        // Expiration Date
   257→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   258→            setCellValue(request.expirationDate.format(DATE_ONLY_FORMATTER))
   259→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   260→        }
   261→
   262→        // Auto-Approved
   263→        row.createCell(colNum++).apply {
   264→            setCellValue(if (request.autoApproved) "Yes" else "No")
   265→            cellStyle = normalStyle
   266→        }
   267→    }
   268→
   269→    /**
   270→     * Set fixed column widths for consistent appearance.
   271→     */
   272→    private fun setColumnWidths(sheet: Sheet) {
   273→        sheet.setColumnWidth(0, 3000)  // Request ID
   274→        sheet.setColumnWidth(1, 5000)  // CVE ID
   275→        sheet.setColumnWidth(2, 8000)  // Asset Name
   276→        sheet.setColumnWidth(3, 4000)  // Asset IP
   277→        sheet.setColumnWidth(4, 5000)  // Requester
   278→        sheet.setColumnWidth(5, 5500)  // Submission Date
   279→        sheet.setColumnWidth(6, 3500)  // Status
   280→        sheet.setColumnWidth(7, 5000)  // Reviewer
   281→        sheet.setColumnWidth(8, 5500)  // Review Date
   282→        sheet.setColumnWidth(9, 12000) // Reason
   283→        sheet.setColumnWidth(10, 12000) // Review Comment
   284→        sheet.setColumnWidth(11, 4000) // Expiration Date
   285→        sheet.setColumnWidth(12, 4000) // Auto-Approved
   286→    }
   287→
   288→    /**
   289→     * Create cell styles for header and normal cells.
   290→     *
   291→     * IMPORTANT: Create styles once and reuse to avoid exceeding 64K style limit.
   292→     */
   293→    private fun createStyles(workbook: Workbook): ExportStyles {
   294→        // Header style (bold, gray background)
   295→        val headerFont = workbook.createFont().apply {
   296→            bold = true
   297→            fontHeightInPoints = 11
   298→        }
   299→        val headerStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   300→            setFont(headerFont)
   301→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.GREY_25_PERCENT.index
   302→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   303→            borderBottom = BorderStyle.THIN
   304→            borderTop = BorderStyle.THIN
   305→            borderLeft = BorderStyle.THIN
   306→            borderRight = BorderStyle.THIN
   307→            alignment = HorizontalAlignment.CENTER
   308→            verticalAlignment = VerticalAlignment.CENTER
   309→            wrapText = false
   310→        }
   311→
   312→        // Normal cell style
   313→        val normalStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   314→            borderBottom = BorderStyle.THIN
   315→            borderTop = BorderStyle.THIN
   316→            borderLeft = BorderStyle.THIN
   317→            borderRight = BorderStyle.THIN
   318→            alignment = HorizontalAlignment.LEFT
   319→            verticalAlignment = VerticalAlignment.TOP
   320→            wrapText = true
   321→        }
   322→
   323→        return ExportStyles(headerStyle, normalStyle)
   324→    }
   325→
   326→    /**
   327→     * Container for reusable cell styles.
   328→     */
   329→    private data class ExportStyles(
   330→        val header: CellStyle,
   331→        val normal: CellStyle
   332→    )
   333→}
   334→

data.prompt:
Explore existing Excel export patterns in this Kotlin/Micronaut backend. I need to understand:

1. How Excel exports are currently implemented - look for Apache POI usage, SXSSFWorkbook, XSSFWorkbook
2. Any export service classes or utilities
3. How existing export endpoints return Excel files (content-type headers, response handling)
4. The pattern used for streaming exports vs in-memory exports

Search in:
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/service/ for any export-related services
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/controller/ for export endpoints
- build.gradle.kts files for Apache POI dependencies
- Look for patterns like "application/vnd.openxmlformats" or "xlsx" or "SXSSFWorkbook" or "XSSFWorkbook"

Be very thorough - I need to understand the exact patterns used so I can replicate them.

content.content:
1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.config.MemoryOptimizationConfig
     4→import com.secman.dto.VulnerabilityExportDto
     5→import com.secman.dto.VulnerabilityWithExceptionDto
     6→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.Authentication
     7→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
     8→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.CellStyle
     9→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.FillPatternType
    10→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.IndexedColors
    11→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Workbook
    12→import org.apache.poi.xssf.streaming.SXSSFWorkbook
    13→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    14→import java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream
    15→import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter
    16→
    17→/**
    18→ * Service for exporting vulnerabilities to Excel format
    19→ * Feature: Vulnerability Export Performance Optimization
    20→ * Feature: 073-memory-optimization (streaming exports)
    21→ *
    22→ * Replaces client-side Excel generation with server-side streaming export.
    23→ * For 358k vulnerabilities:
    24→ * - Old: 715+ API calls, 90-120+ seconds
    25→ * - New: 1 API call, 5-15 seconds
    26→ *
    27→ * Technical Approach (same as AssetExportService):
    28→ * - Use SXSSFWorkbook(100) for streaming export (constant memory)
    29→ * - Create CellStyle objects ONCE before loop (not per cell)
    30→ * - Use fixed column widths (auto-sizing adds 3 minutes for 10K rows)
    31→ * - Dispose workbook in finally block to clean up temp files
    32→ * - Severity color-coding (Critical=Red, High=Orange, Medium=Blue, Low=Green)
    33→ *
    34→ * Memory Optimization (Feature 073):
    35→ * - When streamingExportsEnabled=true: Write directly to Excel during fetch (write-on-fetch)
    36→ * - When streamingExportsEnabled=false: Accumulate all records first (original behavior)
    37→ * - Controlled by MEMORY_STREAMING_EXPORTS environment variable
    38→ */
    39→@Singleton
    40→class VulnerabilityExportService(
    41→    private val vulnerabilityService: VulnerabilityService,
    42→    private val assetFilterService: AssetFilterService,
    43→    private val memoryConfig: MemoryOptimizationConfig
    44→) {
    45→
    46→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityExportService::class.java)
    47→
    48→    // Date formatter for Excel cells
    49→    private val dateFormatter = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
    50→
    51→    /**
    52→     * Export vulnerabilities accessible to the authenticated user
    53→     * Follows same access control pattern as VulnerabilityManagementController
    54→     *
    55→     * Note: This method is kept for backward compatibility when streaming is disabled.
    56→     * When streamingExportsEnabled=true, use exportVulnerabilitiesToExcelStreaming() instead.
    57→     *
    58→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
    59→     * @return List of VulnerabilityExportDto ready for Excel serialization
    60→     */
    61→    fun exportVulnerabilities(authentication: Authentication): List<VulnerabilityExportDto> {
    62→        log.info("Exporting vulnerabilities for user: {} (buffered mode)", authentication.name)
    63→
    64→        val isAdmin = authentication.roles.contains("ADMIN")
    65→
    66→        // Get accessible asset IDs (only needed for non-ADMIN users)
    67→        val accessibleAssetIds = if (isAdmin) {
    68→            emptySet()
    69→        } else {
    70→            assetFilterService.getAccessibleAssets(authentication)
    71→                .mapNotNull { it.id }
    72→                .toSet()
    73→        }
    74→
    75→        // Use optimized query to get ALL vulnerabilities (no pagination for export)
    76→        val allVulnerabilities = mutableListOf<VulnerabilityWithExceptionDto>()
    77→        var page = 0
    78→        val batchSize = memoryConfig.batchSize
    79→        var hasMore = true
    80→
    81→        while (hasMore) {
    82→            val response = vulnerabilityService.getCurrentVulnerabilitiesOptimized(
    83→                accessibleAssetIds = accessibleAssetIds,
    84→                isAdmin = isAdmin,
    85→                severity = null,
    86→                system = null,
    87→                exceptionStatus = null,
    88→                product = null,
    89→                adDomain = null,
    90→                cloudAccountId = null,
    91→                page = page,
    92→                size = batchSize
    93→            )
    94→            allVulnerabilities.addAll(response.content)
    95→            hasMore = response.hasNext
    96→            page++
    97→
    98→            log.debug("Fetched page {} with {} vulnerabilities (total so far: {})",
    99→                page, response.content.size, allVulnerabilities.size)
   100→        }
   101→
   102→        log.info("Found {} vulnerabilities accessible to user {}",
   103→            allVulnerabilities.size, authentication.name)
   104→
   105→        // Convert to export DTOs
   106→        return allVulnerabilities.map { VulnerabilityExportDto.fromVulnerabilityWithException(it) }
   107→    }
   108→
   109→    /**
   110→     * Export vulnerabilities directly to Excel using streaming write-on-fetch pattern
   111→     * Feature: 073-memory-optimization
   112→     *
   113→     * Memory optimization: Instead of accumulating all records then writing,
   114→     * this method writes each batch directly to the Excel workbook during fetch.
   115→     * This reduces peak memory from O(n) to O(batch_size).
   116→     *
   117→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
   118→     * @return ByteArrayOutputStream containing the Excel workbook
   119→     */
   120→    fun exportVulnerabilitiesToExcelStreaming(authentication: Authentication): ByteArrayOutputStream {
   121→        log.info("Exporting vulnerabilities for user: {} (streaming mode)", authentication.name)
   122→
   123→        val isAdmin = authentication.roles.contains("ADMIN")
   124→
   125→        // Get accessible asset IDs (only needed for non-ADMIN users)
   126→        val accessibleAssetIds = if (isAdmin) {
   127→            emptySet()
   128→        } else {
   129→            assetFilterService.getAccessibleAssets(authentication)
   130→                .mapNotNull { it.id }
   131→                .toSet()
   132→        }
   133→
   134→        // Create streaming workbook with 100-row window
   135→        val workbook = SXSSFWorkbook(100)
   136→        workbook.setCompressTempFiles(true)
   137→
   138→        try {
   139→            val sheet = workbook.createSheet("Vulnerabilities")
   140→
   141→            // Create styles ONCE (not per cell) - critical for performance
   142→            val styles = createVulnerabilityStyles(workbook)
   143→
   144→            // Write header row
   145→            createHeaderRow(sheet, styles.header)
   146→
   147→            // Stream data directly to Excel during fetch
   148→            var page = 0
   149→            val batchSize = memoryConfig.batchSize
   150→            var hasMore = true
   151→            var rowNum = 1
   152→            var totalCount = 0
   153→
   154→            while (hasMore) {
   155→                val response = vulnerabilityService.getCurrentVulnerabilitiesOptimized(
   156→                    accessibleAssetIds = accessibleAssetIds,
   157→                    isAdmin = isAdmin,
   158→                    severity = null,
   159→                    system = null,
   160→                    exceptionStatus = null,
   161→                    product = null,
   162→                    adDomain = null,
   163→                    cloudAccountId = null,
   164→                    page = page,
   165→                    size = batchSize
   166→                )
   167→
   168→                // Write batch directly to Excel (no intermediate accumulation)
   169→                response.content.forEach { vuln ->
   170→                    val dto = VulnerabilityExportDto.fromVulnerabilityWithException(vuln)
   171→                    createVulnerabilityRow(sheet, rowNum++, dto, styles)
   172→                    totalCount++
   173→                }
   174→
   175→                hasMore = response.hasNext
   176→                page++
   177→
   178→                log.debug("Streamed page {} with {} vulnerabilities to Excel (total so far: {})",
   179→                    page, response.content.size, totalCount)
   180→            }
   181→
   182→            // Set fixed column widths (auto-sizing adds 3 minutes for 10K rows)
   183→            setFixedColumnWidths(sheet)
   184→
   185→            // Write to output stream
   186→            val outputStream = ByteArrayOutputStream()
   187→            workbook.write(outputStream)
   188→
   189→            log.info("Streaming Excel export complete: {} rows written for user {}",
   190→                totalCount, authentication.name)
   191→
   192→            return outputStream
   193→
   194→        } finally {
   195→            // CRITICAL: Close workbook to clean up temp files
   196→            workbook.close()
   197→            log.debug("SXSSFWorkbook closed, temp files cleaned")
   198→        }
   199→    }
   200→
   201→    /**
   202→     * Export vulnerabilities to Excel, using streaming or buffered mode based on configuration
   203→     * Feature: 073-memory-optimization
   204→     *
   205→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
   206→     * @return ByteArrayOutputStream containing the Excel workbook
   207→     */
   208→    fun exportToExcel(authentication: Authentication): ByteArrayOutputStream {
   209→        return if (memoryConfig.streamingExportsEnabled) {
   210→            log.info("Using streaming export mode (memory optimized)")
   211→            exportVulnerabilitiesToExcelStreaming(authentication)
   212→        } else {
   213→            log.info("Using buffered export mode (original behavior)")
   214→            val dtos = exportVulnerabilities(authentication)
   215→            writeToExcel(dtos)
   216→        }
   217→    }
   218→
   219→    /**
   220→     * Write vulnerability export DTOs to Excel workbook
   221→     *
   222→     * Performance optimizations:
   223→     * - SXSSFWorkbook with 100-row memory window
   224→     * - Create styles ONCE before loop
   225→     * - Fixed column widths (not auto-sizing)
   226→     * - Enable GZIP compression for temp files
   227→     * - Severity color-coding for visual clarity
   228→     *
   229→     * @param dtos List of vulnerability export DTOs
   230→     * @return ByteArrayOutputStream containing Excel workbook
   231→     */
   232→    fun writeToExcel(dtos: List<VulnerabilityExportDto>): ByteArrayOutputStream {
   233→        log.info("Writing {} vulnerabilities to Excel format", dtos.size)
   234→
   235→        // Create streaming workbook with 100-row window
   236→        val workbook = SXSSFWorkbook(100)
   237→        workbook.setCompressTempFiles(true)
   238→
   239→        try {
   240→            val sheet = workbook.createSheet("Vulnerabilities")
   241→
   242→            // Create styles ONCE (not per cell) - critical for performance
   243→            val styles = createVulnerabilityStyles(workbook)
   244→
   245→            // Write header row
   246→            createHeaderRow(sheet, styles.header)
   247→
   248→            // Write data rows
   249→            dtos.forEachIndexed { index, dto ->
   250→                createVulnerabilityRow(sheet, index + 1, dto, styles)
   251→            }
   252→
   253→            // Set fixed column widths (auto-sizing adds 3 minutes for 10K rows)
   254→            setFixedColumnWidths(sheet)
   255→
   256→            // Write to output stream
   257→            val outputStream = ByteArrayOutputStream()
   258→            workbook.write(outputStream)
   259→
   260→            log.info("Excel export complete: {} rows written", dtos.size)
   261→
   262→            return outputStream
   263→
   264→        } finally {
   265→            // CRITICAL: Close workbook to clean up temp files
   266→            workbook.close()
   267→            log.debug("SXSSFWorkbook closed, temp files cleaned")
   268→        }
   269→    }
   270→
   271→    /**
   272→     * Create cell styles for Excel export (created ONCE, reused for all cells)
   273→     * Includes severity color-coding: Critical=Red, High=Orange, Medium=Blue, Low=Green
   274→     */
   275→    private fun createVulnerabilityStyles(workbook: Workbook): VulnerabilityStyles {
   276→        // Header style
   277→        val headerStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   278→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.GREY_25_PERCENT.index
   279→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   280→            val font = workbook.createFont()
   281→            font.bold = true
   282→            setFont(font)
   283→        }
   284→
   285→        // Text style (default)
   286→        val textStyle = workbook.createCellStyle()
   287→
   288→        // Severity styles with color-coding
   289→        val criticalStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   290→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.RED.index
   291→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   292→            val font = workbook.createFont()
   293→            font.bold = true
   294→            font.color = IndexedColors.WHITE.index
   295→            setFont(font)
   296→        }
   297→
   298→        val highStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   299→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.ORANGE.index
   300→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   301→            val font = workbook.createFont()
   302→            font.bold = true
   303→            setFont(font)
   304→        }
   305→
   306→        val mediumStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   307→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.LIGHT_BLUE.index
   308→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   309→        }
   310→
   311→        val lowStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   312→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.LIGHT_GREEN.index
   313→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   314→        }
   315→
   316→        // Overdue status styles
   317→        val overdueStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   318→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.CORAL.index
   319→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   320→            val font = workbook.createFont()
   321→            font.bold = true
   322→            setFont(font)
   323→        }
   324→
   325→        val exceptedStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   326→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.LIGHT_YELLOW.index
   327→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   328→        }
   329→
   330→        val okStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   331→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.LIGHT_GREEN.index
   332→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   333→        }
   334→
   335→        // Wrap text style for descriptions
   336→        val wrapTextStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   337→            wrapText = true
   338→        }
   339→
   340→        return VulnerabilityStyles(
   341→            header = headerStyle,
   342→            text = textStyle,
   343→            critical = criticalStyle,
   344→            high = highStyle,
   345→            medium = mediumStyle,
   346→            low = lowStyle,
   347→            overdue = overdueStyle,
   348→            excepted = exceptedStyle,
   349→            ok = okStyle,
   350→            wrapText = wrapTextStyle
   351→        )
   352→    }
   353→
   354→    /**
   355→     * Create header row with column names
   356→     */
   357→    private fun createHeaderRow(sheet: org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Sheet, headerStyle: CellStyle) {
   358→        val headerRow = sheet.createRow(0)
   359→        val headers = listOf(
   360→            "Asset Name", "IP", "CVE ID", "Severity", "Product",
   361→            "Days Open", "Scan Date", "Overdue Status", "Has Exception", "Exception Reason"
   362→        )
   363→
   364→        headers.forEachIndexed { index, header ->
   365→            val cell = headerRow.createCell(index)
   366→            cell.setCellValue(header)
   367→            cell.cellStyle = headerStyle
   368→        }
   369→    }
   370→
   371→    /**
   372→     * Create data row for single vulnerability
   373→     */
   374→    private fun createVulnerabilityRow(
   375→        sheet: org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Sheet,
   376→        rowNum: Int,
   377→        dto: VulnerabilityExportDto,
   378→        styles: VulnerabilityStyles
   379→    ) {
   380→        val row = sheet.createRow(rowNum)
   381→
   382→        // Asset Name
   383→        row.createCell(0).apply {
   384→            setCellValue(dto.assetName)
   385→            cellStyle = styles.text
   386→        }
   387→
   388→        // IP
   389→        row.createCell(1).apply {
   390→            setCellValue(dto.assetIp ?: "")
   391→            cellStyle = styles.text
   392→        }
   393→
   394→        // CVE ID
   395→        row.createCell(2).apply {
   396→            setCellValue(dto.cveId ?: "")
   397→            cellStyle = styles.text
   398→        }
   399→
   400→        // Severity (with color-coding)
   401→        row.createCell(3).apply {
   402→            setCellValue(dto.severity ?: "")
   403→            cellStyle = when (dto.severity?.lowercase()) {
   404→                "critical" -> styles.critical
   405→                "high" -> styles.high
   406→                "medium" -> styles.medium
   407→                "low" -> styles.low
   408→                else -> styles.text
   409→            }
   410→        }
   411→
   412→        // Product
   413→        row.createCell(4).apply {
   414→            setCellValue(dto.product ?: "")
   415→            cellStyle = styles.wrapText
   416→        }
   417→
   418→        // Days Open
   419→        row.createCell(5).apply {
   420→            setCellValue(dto.daysOpen ?: "")
   421→            cellStyle = styles.text
   422→        }
   423→
   424→        // Scan Date
   425→        row.createCell(6).apply {
   426→            setCellValue(dto.scanDate.format(dateFormatter))
   427→            cellStyle = styles.text
   428→        }
   429→
   430→        // Overdue Status (with color-coding)
   431→        row.createCell(7).apply {
   432→            setCellValue(dto.overdueStatus)
   433→            cellStyle = when (dto.overdueStatus.uppercase()) {
   434→                "OVERDUE" -> styles.overdue
   435→                "EXCEPTED" -> styles.excepted
   436→                "OK" -> styles.ok
   437→                else -> styles.text
   438→            }
   439→        }
   440→
   441→        // Has Exception
   442→        row.createCell(8).apply {
   443→            setCellValue(if (dto.hasException) "Yes" else "No")
   444→            cellStyle = styles.text
   445→        }
   446→
   447→        // Exception Reason
   448→        row.createCell(9).apply {
   449→            setCellValue(dto.exceptionReason ?: "")
   450→            cellStyle = styles.wrapText
   451→        }
   452→    }
   453→
   454→    /**
   455→     * Set fixed column widths (auto-sizing adds 3 minutes overhead for 10K rows)
   456→     */
   457→    private fun setFixedColumnWidths(sheet: org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Sheet) {
   458→        sheet.setColumnWidth(0, 7000)  // Asset Name
   459→        sheet.setColumnWidth(1, 4000)  // IP
   460→        sheet.setColumnWidth(2, 5000)  // CVE ID
   461→        sheet.setColumnWidth(3, 3000)  // Severity
   462→        sheet.setColumnWidth(4, 10000) // Product
   463→        sheet.setColumnWidth(5, 3500)  // Days Open
   464→        sheet.setColumnWidth(6, 5500)  // Scan Date
   465→        sheet.setColumnWidth(7, 4000)  // Overdue Status
   466→        sheet.setColumnWidth(8, 4000)  // Has Exception
   467→        sheet.setColumnWidth(9, 10000) // Exception Reason
   468→    }
   469→
   470→    /**
   471→     * Data class for style objects (created once, reused)
   472→     */
   473→    private data class VulnerabilityStyles(
   474→        val header: CellStyle,
   475→        val text: CellStyle,
   476→        val critical: CellStyle,
   477→        val high: CellStyle,
   478→        val medium: CellStyle,
   479→        val low: CellStyle,
   480→        val overdue: CellStyle,
   481→        val excepted: CellStyle,
   482→        val ok: CellStyle,
   483→        val wrapText: CellStyle
   484→    )
   485→}
   486→

2. Any export service classes or utilities
3. How existing export endpoints return Excel files (content-type headers, response handling)
4. The pattern used for streaming exports vs in-memory exports

Search in:
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/service/ for any export-related services
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/controller/ for export endpoints
- build.gradle.kts files for Apache POI dependencies
- Look for patterns like "application/vnd.openxmlformats" or "xlsx" or "SXSSFWorkbook" or "XSSFWorkbook"

Be very thorough - I need to understand the exact patterns used so I can replicate them.
