1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.config.MemoryOptimizationConfig
     4→import com.secman.domain.ExportJob
     5→import com.secman.domain.ExportJobStatus
     6→import com.secman.domain.ExportType
     7→import com.secman.dto.ExportJobDto
     8→import com.secman.dto.VulnerabilityExportDto
     9→import com.secman.repository.ExportJobRepository
    10→import io.micronaut.context.annotation.Value
    11→import io.micronaut.scheduling.TaskExecutors
    12→import io.micronaut.scheduling.annotation.Scheduled
    13→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.Authentication
    14→import jakarta.inject.Named
    15→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    16→import jakarta.persistence.EntityManager
    17→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
    18→import java.util.concurrent.ExecutorService
    19→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.CellStyle
    20→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.FillPatternType
    21→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.IndexedColors
    22→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Workbook
    23→import org.apache.poi.xssf.streaming.SXSSFWorkbook
    24→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    25→import java.io.File
    26→import java.io.FileOutputStream
    27→import java.time.LocalDate
    28→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    29→import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter
    30→import java.util.*
    31→
    32→/**
    33→ * Service for managing background export jobs
    34→ * Feature: Vulnerability Export Performance Optimization - Background Job Pattern
    35→ * Feature: 073-memory-optimization (configurable batch size)
    36→ *
    37→ * Handles:
    38→ * - Starting export jobs asynchronously
    39→ * - Tracking progress
    40→ * - Writing Excel files to temporary storage
    41→ * - Cleanup of old export files
    42→ *
    43→ * Rate Limiting:
    44→ * - Max 1 concurrent export per user
    45→ * - Max 3 concurrent exports globally
    46→ *
    47→ * Memory Optimization (Feature 073):
    48→ * - Uses SXSSFWorkbook with 100-row memory window (streaming write)
    49→ * - Writes directly to Excel during fetch (write-on-fetch pattern)
    50→ * - Configurable batch size via MEMORY_BATCH_SIZE environment variable
    51→ */
    52→@Singleton
    53→open class ExportJobService(
    54→    private val exportJobRepository: ExportJobRepository,
    55→    private val vulnerabilityService: VulnerabilityService,
    56→    private val assetFilterService: AssetFilterService,
    57→    @Named(TaskExecutors.IO) private val executorService: ExecutorService,
    58→    private val entityManager: EntityManager,
    59→    private val memoryConfig: MemoryOptimizationConfig,
    60→    @Value("\${secman.export.directory:exports}") private val exportDirectory: String,
    61→    @Value("\${secman.export.max-concurrent-per-user:1}") private val maxConcurrentPerUser: Int,
    62→    @Value("\${secman.export.max-concurrent-global:3}") private val maxConcurrentGlobal: Int,
    63→    @Value("\${secman.export.file-retention-hours:24}") private val fileRetentionHours: Long
    64→) {
    65→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ExportJobService::class.java)
    66→    private val dateFormatter = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
    67→
    68→    init {
    69→        // Ensure export directory exists
    70→        val exportDir = File(exportDirectory)
    71→        if (!exportDir.exists()) {
    72→            exportDir.mkdirs()
    73→            log.info("Created export directory: {}", exportDir.absolutePath)
    74→        }
    75→    }
    76→
    77→    /**
    78→     * Start a new export job
    79→     *
    80→     * @param authentication Current user authentication
    81→     * @param exportType Type of export (default: VULNERABILITIES)
    82→     * @return Created job DTO
    83→     * @throws IllegalStateException if rate limits exceeded
    84→     */
    85→    @Transactional
    86→    open fun startExport(authentication: Authentication, exportType: ExportType = ExportType.VULNERABILITIES): ExportJobDto {
    87→        val username = authentication.name
    88→        log.info("Starting export job for user: {}, type: {}", username, exportType)
    89→
    90→        // Check rate limits
    91→        val runningStatuses = listOf(ExportJobStatus.PENDING, ExportJobStatus.PROCESSING)
    92→
    93→        val userRunningJobs = exportJobRepository.countByUsernameAndStatusIn(username, runningStatuses)
    94→        if (userRunningJobs >= maxConcurrentPerUser) {
    95→            log.warn("User {} already has {} running export(s), max allowed: {}", username, userRunningJobs, maxConcurrentPerUser)
    96→            throw IllegalStateException("You already have an export in progress. Please wait for it to complete.")
    97→        }
    98→
    99→        val globalRunningJobs = exportJobRepository.countByStatusIn(runningStatuses)
   100→        if (globalRunningJobs >= maxConcurrentGlobal) {
   101→            log.warn("Global export limit reached: {} running, max allowed: {}", globalRunningJobs, maxConcurrentGlobal)
   102→            throw IllegalStateException("Server is busy with exports. Please try again in a few minutes.")
   103→        }
   104→
   105→        // Create job
   106→        val jobId = UUID.randomUUID().toString()
   107→        val job = ExportJob(
   108→            id = jobId,
   109→            username = username,
   110→            status = ExportJobStatus.PENDING,
   111→            exportType = exportType
   112→        )
   113→
   114→        val savedJob = exportJobRepository.save(job)
   115→        // Force immediate flush to avoid MariaDB JDBC driver batching bug
   116→        // (IndexOutOfBoundsException in handleStandardResults)
   117→        entityManager.flush()
   118→        log.info("Created export job: {}", jobId)
   119→
   120→        // Start async processing using ExecutorService
   121→        // We need to capture the authentication info since Authentication may not be available in background thread
   122→        val isAdmin = authentication.roles.contains("ADMIN")
   123→        val accessibleAssetIds = if (isAdmin) {
   124→            emptySet()
   125→        } else {
   126→            assetFilterService.getAccessibleAssets(authentication)
   127→                .mapNotNull { it.id }
   128→                .toSet()
   129→        }
   130→
   131→        executorService.submit {
   132→            processExportInBackground(jobId, username, isAdmin, accessibleAssetIds)
   133→        }
   134→
   135→        return ExportJobDto.fromEntity(savedJob)
   136→    }
   137→
   138→    /**
   139→     * Get export job status
   140→     *
   141→     * @param jobId Job ID
   142→     * @param username Username for authorization
   143→     * @return Job DTO or null if not found
   144→     */
   145→    fun getJobStatus(jobId: String, username: String): ExportJobDto? {
   146→        val job = exportJobRepository.findByIdAndUsername(jobId, username).orElse(null)
   147→        return job?.let { ExportJobDto.fromEntity(it) }
   148→    }
   149→
   150→    /**
   151→     * Get export file for download
   152→     *
   153→     * @param jobId Job ID
   154→     * @param username Username for authorization
   155→     * @return File or null if not found/not ready
   156→     */
   157→    fun getExportFile(jobId: String, username: String): File? {
   158→        val job = exportJobRepository.findByIdAndUsername(jobId, username).orElse(null)
   159→
   160→        if (job == null) {
   161→            log.warn("Job not found: {}", jobId)
   162→            return null
   163→        }
   164→
   165→        if (!job.isDownloadable()) {
   166→            log.warn("Job {} is not downloadable, status: {}", jobId, job.status)
   167→            return null
   168→        }
   169→
   170→        val file = File(job.filePath!!)
   171→        if (!file.exists()) {
   172→            log.error("Export file not found: {}", job.filePath)
   173→            // Mark job as expired
   174→            job.status = ExportJobStatus.EXPIRED
   175→            job.errorMessage = "Export file was cleaned up"
   176→            exportJobRepository.update(job)
   177→            return null
   178→        }
   179→
   180→        return file
   181→    }
   182→
   183→    /**
   184→     * Get recent export jobs for a user
   185→     */
   186→    fun getRecentJobs(username: String, limit: Int = 10): List<ExportJobDto> {
   187→        return exportJobRepository.findByUsernameOrderByCreatedAtDesc(username)
   188→            .take(limit)
   189→            .map { ExportJobDto.fromEntity(it) }
   190→    }
   191→
   192→    /**
   193→     * Cancel a running export job
   194→     */
   195→    @Transactional
   196→    open fun cancelJob(jobId: String, username: String): Boolean {
   197→        val job = exportJobRepository.findByIdAndUsername(jobId, username).orElse(null) ?: return false
   198→
   199→        if (!job.isRunning()) {
   200→            log.warn("Cannot cancel job {} - not running (status: {})", jobId, job.status)
   201→            return false
   202→        }
   203→
   204→        job.status = ExportJobStatus.CANCELLED
   205→        job.completedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   206→        job.errorMessage = "Cancelled by user"
   207→        exportJobRepository.update(job)
   208→
   209→        log.info("Cancelled export job: {}", jobId)
   210→        return true
   211→    }
   212→
   213→    /**
   214→     * Reset all stuck jobs for a user
   215→     * Useful when jobs get stuck in PENDING/PROCESSING status due to server restart or errors
   216→     *
   217→     * @param username Username to reset jobs for
   218→     * @return Number of jobs reset
   219→     */
   220→    @Transactional
   221→    open fun resetStuckJobs(username: String): Int {
   222→        val runningStatuses = listOf(ExportJobStatus.PENDING, ExportJobStatus.PROCESSING)
   223→        val stuckJobs = exportJobRepository.findByUsernameAndStatusIn(username, runningStatuses)
   224→
   225→        if (stuckJobs.isEmpty()) {
   226→            log.info("No stuck jobs found for user: {}", username)
   227→            return 0
   228→        }
   229→
   230→        stuckJobs.forEach { job ->
   231→            log.info("Resetting stuck job: {} (status: {}, created: {})", job.id, job.status, job.createdAt)
   232→            job.status = ExportJobStatus.FAILED
   233→            job.completedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   234→            job.errorMessage = "Reset by user - job was stuck"
   235→            exportJobRepository.update(job)
   236→        }
   237→
   238→        log.info("Reset {} stuck jobs for user: {}", stuckJobs.size, username)
   239→        return stuckJobs.size
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    /**
   243→     * Process export in background thread
   244→     * Called via ExecutorService to ensure true async execution
   245→     *
   246→     * Uses separate @Transactional methods (not self-invocation) to ensure
   247→     * proper Hibernate Session management in background threads.
   248→     * Each database operation runs in its own short transaction.
   249→     *
   250→     * @param jobId Job ID to process
   251→     * @param username Username for logging
   252→     * @param isAdmin Whether user is admin (for access control)
   253→     * @param accessibleAssetIds Pre-computed set of accessible asset IDs
   254→     */
   255→    private fun processExportInBackground(
   256→        jobId: String,
   257→        username: String,
   258→        isAdmin: Boolean,
   259→        accessibleAssetIds: Set<Long>
   260→    ) {
   261→        log.info("Starting background processing for job: {} (user: {})", jobId, username)
   262→
   263→        try {
   264→            // Load job in its own transaction
   265→            val job = loadJobForProcessing(jobId)
   266→            if (job == null) {
   267→                log.error("Job not found for background processing: {}", jobId)
   268→                return
   269→            }
   270→
   271→            // Check if cancelled before starting
   272→            if (job.status == ExportJobStatus.CANCELLED) {
   273→                log.info("Job {} was cancelled before processing started", jobId)
   274→                return
   275→            }
   276→
   277→            // Update status to processing (separate transaction)
   278→            markJobAsProcessing(jobId)
   279→
   280→            when (job.exportType) {
   281→                ExportType.VULNERABILITIES -> processVulnerabilityExport(jobId, isAdmin, accessibleAssetIds)
   282→                else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("Unsupported export type: ${job.exportType}")
   283→            }
   284→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   285→            log.error("Export job {} failed", jobId, e)
   286→            // Update job status in a separate transaction
   287→            try {
   288→                markJobAsFailed(jobId, e.message?.take(1000) ?: "Unknown error")
   289→            } catch (updateEx: Exception) {
   290→                log.error("Failed to update job {} status after error", jobId, updateEx)
   291→            }
   292→        }
   293→    }
   294→
   295→    // ============================================================
   296→    // Transactional helper methods for background thread operations
   297→    // These methods run in their own transactions via AOP proxy
   298→    // ============================================================
   299→
   300→    /**
   301→     * Load a job by ID in its own transaction
   302→     */
   303→    @Transactional
   304→    open fun loadJobForProcessing(jobId: String): ExportJob? {
   305→        return exportJobRepository.findById(jobId).orElse(null)
   306→    }
   307→
   308→    /**
   309→     * Mark a job as processing
   310→     */
   311→    @Transactional
   312→    open fun markJobAsProcessing(jobId: String) {
   313→        val job = exportJobRepository.findById(jobId).orElse(null) ?: return
   314→        job.status = ExportJobStatus.PROCESSING
   315→        job.startedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   316→        exportJobRepository.update(job)
   317→        entityManager.flush()
   318→    }
   319→
   320→    /**
   321→     * Check if a job has been cancelled
   322→     */
   323→    @Transactional
   324→    open fun isJobCancelled(jobId: String): Boolean {
   325→        val job = exportJobRepository.findById(jobId).orElse(null)
   326→        return job?.status == ExportJobStatus.CANCELLED
   327→    }
   328→
   329→    /**
   330→     * Update job total items count
   331→     */
   332→    @Transactional
   333→    open fun updateJobTotalItems(jobId: String, totalItems: Long) {
   334→        val job = exportJobRepository.findById(jobId).orElse(null) ?: return
   335→        job.totalItems = totalItems
   336→        exportJobRepository.update(job)
   337→        entityManager.flush()
   338→    }
   339→
   340→    /**
   341→     * Update job progress
   342→     */
   343→    @Transactional
   344→    open fun updateJobProgress(jobId: String, processedItems: Long) {
   345→        val job = exportJobRepository.findById(jobId).orElse(null) ?: return
   346→        job.processedItems = processedItems
   347→        exportJobRepository.update(job)
   348→        entityManager.flush()
   349→    }
   350→
   351→    /**
   352→     * Mark a job as completed with file info
   353→     */
   354→    @Transactional
   355→    open fun markJobAsCompleted(jobId: String, filePath: String, fileName: String, fileSizeBytes: Long, totalItems: Long) {
   356→        val job = exportJobRepository.findById(jobId).orElse(null) ?: return
   357→        job.status = ExportJobStatus.COMPLETED
   358→        job.completedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   359→        job.filePath = filePath
   360→        job.fileName = fileName
   361→        job.fileSizeBytes = fileSizeBytes
   362→        job.processedItems = totalItems
   363→        exportJobRepository.update(job)
   364→        entityManager.flush()
   365→    }
   366→
   367→    /**
   368→     * Mark a job as failed
   369→     */
   370→    @Transactional
   371→    open fun markJobAsFailed(jobId: String, errorMessage: String) {
   372→        val job = exportJobRepository.findById(jobId).orElse(null) ?: return
   373→        job.status = ExportJobStatus.FAILED
   374→        job.completedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   375→        job.errorMessage = errorMessage
   376→        exportJobRepository.update(job)
   377→        entityManager.flush()
   378→    }
   379→
   380→    /**
   381→     * Process vulnerability export
   382→     * Uses jobId and calls transactional helper methods for all DB operations
   383→     */
   384→    private fun processVulnerabilityExport(
   385→        jobId: String,
   386→        isAdmin: Boolean,
   387→        accessibleAssetIds: Set<Long>
   388→    ) {
   389→        // First, get total count for progress tracking
   390→        val firstPage = vulnerabilityService.getCurrentVulnerabilitiesOptimized(
   391→            accessibleAssetIds = accessibleAssetIds,
   392→            isAdmin = isAdmin,
   393→            severity = null,
   394→            system = null,
   395→            exceptionStatus = null,
   396→            product = null,
   397→            adDomain = null,
   398→            cloudAccountId = null,
   399→            page = 0,
   400→            size = 1
   401→        )
   402→        val totalItems = firstPage.totalElements
   403→        updateJobTotalItems(jobId, totalItems)
   404→
   405→        log.info("Job {}: Total items to export: {}", jobId, totalItems)
   406→
   407→        // Create output file
   408→        val dateStr = LocalDate.now().format(DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE)
   409→        val fileName = "vulnerabilities_export_${dateStr}_${jobId.take(8)}.xlsx"
   410→        val filePath = "$exportDirectory/$fileName"
   411→
   412→        // Create streaming workbook and write directly to file
   413→        val workbook = SXSSFWorkbook(100)
   414→        workbook.setCompressTempFiles(true)
   415→        val startTime = LocalDateTime.now()
   416→
   417→        try {
   418→            val sheet = workbook.createSheet("Vulnerabilities")
   419→            val styles = createVulnerabilityStyles(workbook)
   420→            createHeaderRow(sheet, styles)
   421→
   422→            var rowNum = 1
   423→            var page = 0
   424→            val batchSize = memoryConfig.batchSize
   425→            var hasMore = true
   426→            var lastProgressUpdate = System.currentTimeMillis()
   427→            var processedItems = 0L
   428→
   429→            while (hasMore) {
   430→                // Check for cancellation (separate transaction)
   431→                if (isJobCancelled(jobId)) {
   432→                    log.info("Job {} was cancelled during processing", jobId)
   433→                    workbook.close()
   434→                    return
   435→                }
   436→
   437→                val response = vulnerabilityService.getCurrentVulnerabilitiesOptimized(
   438→                    accessibleAssetIds = accessibleAssetIds,
   439→                    isAdmin = isAdmin,
   440→                    severity = null,
   441→                    system = null,
   442→                    exceptionStatus = null,
   443→                    product = null,
   444→                    adDomain = null,
   445→                    cloudAccountId = null,
   446→                    page = page,
   447→                    size = batchSize
   448→                )
   449→
   450→                response.content.forEach { vuln ->
   451→                    val dto = VulnerabilityExportDto.fromVulnerabilityWithException(vuln)
   452→                    createVulnerabilityRow(sheet, rowNum++, dto, styles)
   453→                }
   454→
   455→                processedItems = (page + 1).toLong() * batchSize
   456→                if (processedItems > totalItems) {
   457→                    processedItems = totalItems
   458→                }
   459→
   460→                // Update progress every 2 seconds or every 10k rows (separate transaction)
   461→                val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
   462→                if (now - lastProgressUpdate > 2000 || processedItems % 10000 == 0L) {
   463→                    updateJobProgress(jobId, processedItems)
   464→                    lastProgressUpdate = now
   465→                    val progressPercent = if (totalItems > 0) (processedItems * 100 / totalItems) else 0
   466→                    log.debug("Job {}: Progress {}% ({}/{})", jobId, progressPercent, processedItems, totalItems)
   467→                }
   468→
   469→                hasMore = response.hasNext
   470→                page++
   471→            }
   472→
   473→            // Set column widths
   474→            setFixedColumnWidths(sheet)
   475→
   476→            // Write to file
   477→            FileOutputStream(filePath).use { fos ->
   478→                workbook.write(fos)
   479→            }
   480→
   481→            // Update job with file info (separate transaction)
   482→            val file = File(filePath)
   483→            markJobAsCompleted(jobId, filePath, fileName, file.length(), totalItems)
   484→
   485→            val completedTime = LocalDateTime.now()
   486→            log.info("Job {} completed: {} rows, {} bytes, took {}ms",
   487→                jobId, rowNum - 1, file.length(),
   488→                java.time.Duration.between(startTime, completedTime).toMillis())
   489→
   490→        } finally {
   491→            workbook.close()
   492→        }
   493→    }
   494→
   495→    /**
   496→     * Scheduled cleanup of old export files
   497→     * Runs every hour
   498→     */
   499→    @Scheduled(fixedDelay = "1h", initialDelay = "5m")
   500→    open fun cleanupOldExports() {
   501→        log.info("Starting export file cleanup (retention: {} hours)", fileRetentionHours)
   502→
   503→        val cutoffDate = LocalDateTime.now().minusHours(fileRetentionHours)
   504→
   505→        // Find completed jobs older than retention period
   506→        val oldJobs = exportJobRepository.findByStatusAndCompletedAtBefore(
   507→            ExportJobStatus.COMPLETED, cutoffDate
   508→        )
   509→
   510→        var filesDeleted = 0
   511→        var bytesFreed = 0L
   512→
   513→        oldJobs.forEach { job ->
   514→            try {
   515→                job.filePath?.let { path ->
   516→                    val file = File(path)
   517→                    if (file.exists()) {
   518→                        bytesFreed += file.length()
   519→                        file.delete()
   520→                        filesDeleted++
   521→                    }
   522→                }
   523→                job.status = ExportJobStatus.EXPIRED
   524→                job.filePath = null
   525→                job.errorMessage = "File cleaned up after ${fileRetentionHours}h retention"
   526→                exportJobRepository.update(job)
   527→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   528→                log.error("Failed to cleanup job {}: {}", job.id, e.message)
   529→            }
   530→        }
   531→
   532→        // Also cleanup failed/cancelled jobs older than 7 days
   533→        val oldFailedJobs = exportJobRepository.findByCreatedAtBefore(
   534→            LocalDateTime.now().minusDays(7)
   535→        ).filter { it.status in listOf(ExportJobStatus.FAILED, ExportJobStatus.CANCELLED, ExportJobStatus.EXPIRED) }
   536→
   537→        oldFailedJobs.forEach { job ->
   538→            exportJobRepository.delete(job)
   539→        }
   540→
   541→        if (filesDeleted > 0 || oldFailedJobs.isNotEmpty()) {
   542→            log.info("Cleanup complete: {} files deleted ({} bytes freed), {} old job records removed",
   543→                filesDeleted, bytesFreed, oldFailedJobs.size)
   544→        }
   545→    }
   546→
   547→    // Excel generation helper methods (same as VulnerabilityExportService)
   548→
   549→    private data class VulnerabilityStyles(
   550→        val header: CellStyle,
   551→        val text: CellStyle,
   552→        val critical: CellStyle,
   553→        val high: CellStyle,
   554→        val medium: CellStyle,
   555→        val low: CellStyle,
   556→        val overdue: CellStyle,
   557→        val excepted: CellStyle,
   558→        val ok: CellStyle,
   559→        val wrapText: CellStyle
   560→    )
   561→
   562→    private fun createVulnerabilityStyles(workbook: Workbook): VulnerabilityStyles {
   563→        val headerStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   564→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.GREY_25_PERCENT.index
   565→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   566→            val font = workbook.createFont()
   567→            font.bold = true
   568→            setFont(font)
   569→        }
   570→
   571→        val textStyle = workbook.createCellStyle()
   572→
   573→        val criticalStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   574→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.RED.index
   575→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   576→            val font = workbook.createFont()
   577→            font.bold = true
   578→            font.color = IndexedColors.WHITE.index
   579→            setFont(font)
   580→        }
   581→
   582→        val highStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   583→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.ORANGE.index
   584→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   585→            val font = workbook.createFont()
   586→            font.bold = true
   587→            setFont(font)
   588→        }
   589→
   590→        val mediumStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   591→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.LIGHT_BLUE.index
   592→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   593→        }
   594→
   595→        val lowStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   596→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.LIGHT_GREEN.index
   597→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   598→        }
   599→
   600→        val overdueStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   601→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.CORAL.index
   602→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   603→            val font = workbook.createFont()
   604→            font.bold = true
   605→            setFont(font)
   606→        }
   607→
   608→        val exceptedStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   609→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.LIGHT_YELLOW.index
   610→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   611→        }
   612→
   613→        val okStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   614→            fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.LIGHT_GREEN.index
   615→            fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   616→        }
   617→
   618→        val wrapTextStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   619→            wrapText = true
   620→        }
   621→
   622→        return VulnerabilityStyles(
   623→            header = headerStyle,
   624→            text = textStyle,
   625→            critical = criticalStyle,
   626→            high = highStyle,
   627→            medium = mediumStyle,
   628→            low = lowStyle,
   629→            overdue = overdueStyle,
   630→            excepted = exceptedStyle,
   631→            ok = okStyle,
   632→            wrapText = wrapTextStyle
   633→        )
   634→    }
   635→
   636→    private fun createHeaderRow(sheet: org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Sheet, styles: VulnerabilityStyles) {
   637→        val headerRow = sheet.createRow(0)
   638→        val headers = listOf(
   639→            "Asset Name", "IP", "CVE ID", "Severity", "Product",
   640→            "Days Open", "Scan Date", "Overdue Status", "Has Exception", "Exception Reason"
   641→        )
   642→
   643→        headers.forEachIndexed { index, header ->
   644→            val cell = headerRow.createCell(index)
   645→            cell.setCellValue(header)
   646→            cell.cellStyle = styles.header
   647→        }
   648→    }
   649→
   650→    private fun createVulnerabilityRow(
   651→        sheet: org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Sheet,
   652→        rowNum: Int,
   653→        dto: VulnerabilityExportDto,
   654→        styles: VulnerabilityStyles
   655→    ) {
   656→        val row = sheet.createRow(rowNum)
   657→
   658→        row.createCell(0).apply {
   659→            setCellValue(dto.assetName)
   660→            cellStyle = styles.text
   661→        }
   662→
   663→        row.createCell(1).apply {
   664→            setCellValue(dto.assetIp ?: "")
   665→            cellStyle = styles.text
   666→        }
   667→
   668→        row.createCell(2).apply {
   669→            setCellValue(dto.cveId ?: "")
   670→            cellStyle = styles.text
   671→        }
   672→
   673→        row.createCell(3).apply {
   674→            setCellValue(dto.severity ?: "")
   675→            cellStyle = when (dto.severity?.lowercase()) {
   676→                "critical" -> styles.critical
   677→                "high" -> styles.high
   678→                "medium" -> styles.medium
   679→                "low" -> styles.low
   680→                else -> styles.text
   681→            }
   682→        }
   683→
   684→        row.createCell(4).apply {
   685→            setCellValue(dto.product ?: "")
   686→            cellStyle = styles.wrapText
   687→        }
   688→
   689→        row.createCell(5).apply {
   690→            setCellValue(dto.daysOpen ?: "")
   691→            cellStyle = styles.text
   692→        }
   693→
   694→        row.createCell(6).apply {
   695→            setCellValue(dto.scanDate.format(dateFormatter))
   696→            cellStyle = styles.text
   697→        }
   698→
   699→        row.createCell(7).apply {
   700→            setCellValue(dto.overdueStatus)
   701→            cellStyle = when (dto.overdueStatus.uppercase()) {
   702→                "OVERDUE" -> styles.overdue
   703→                "EXCEPTED" -> styles.excepted
   704→                "OK" -> styles.ok
   705→                else -> styles.text
   706→            }
   707→        }
   708→
   709→        row.createCell(8).apply {
   710→            setCellValue(if (dto.hasException) "Yes" else "No")
   711→            cellStyle = styles.text
   712→        }
   713→
   714→        row.createCell(9).apply {
   715→            setCellValue(dto.exceptionReason ?: "")
   716→            cellStyle = styles.wrapText
   717→        }
   718→    }
   719→
   720→    private fun setFixedColumnWidths(sheet: org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.Sheet) {
   721→        sheet.setColumnWidth(0, 7000)  // Asset Name
   722→        sheet.setColumnWidth(1, 4000)  // IP
   723→        sheet.setColumnWidth(2, 5000)  // CVE ID
   724→        sheet.setColumnWidth(3, 3000)  // Severity
   725→        sheet.setColumnWidth(4, 10000) // Product
   726→        sheet.setColumnWidth(5, 3500)  // Days Open
   727→        sheet.setColumnWidth(6, 5500)  // Scan Date
   728→        sheet.setColumnWidth(7, 4000)  // Overdue Status
   729→        sheet.setColumnWidth(8, 4000)  // Has Exception
   730→        sheet.setColumnWidth(9, 10000) // Exception Reason
   731→    }
   732→}
   733→

    10→/**

     3→import com.secman.domain.RequirementReview
     4→import com.secman.domain.RequirementReview.ReviewAssessment
     5→import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query
     6→import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Repository
     7→import io.micronaut.data.jpa.repository.JpaRepository
     8→import java.util.*
     9→
    10→/**
    11→ * Repository for RequirementReview entity.
    12→ * Feature: 068-requirements-alignment-process
    13→ */
    14→@Repository
    15→interface RequirementReviewRepository : JpaRepository<RequirementReview, Long> {
    16→
    17→    /**
    18→     * Find all reviews for a session.
    19→     */
    20→    fun findBySession_Id(sessionId: Long): List<RequirementReview>
    21→
    22→    /**
    23→     * Find all reviews by a specific reviewer.
    24→     */
    25→    fun findByReviewer_Id(reviewerId: Long): List<RequirementReview>
    26→
    27→    /**
    28→     * Find all reviews for a specific snapshot (requirement).
    29→     */
    30→    fun findBySnapshot_Id(snapshotId: Long): List<RequirementReview>
    31→
    32→    /**
    33→     * Find review by reviewer and snapshot (unique).
    34→     */
    35→    fun findByReviewer_IdAndSnapshot_Id(reviewerId: Long, snapshotId: Long): Optional<RequirementReview>
    36→
    37→    /**
    38→     * Count reviews by assessment type for a session.
    39→     */
    40→    fun countBySession_IdAndAssessment(sessionId: Long, assessment: ReviewAssessment): Long
    41→
    42→    /**
    43→     * Count reviews by assessment type for a specific snapshot.
    44→     */
    45→    fun countBySnapshot_IdAndAssessment(snapshotId: Long, assessment: ReviewAssessment): Long
    46→
    47→    /**
    48→     * Count total reviews submitted by a reviewer.
    49→     */
    50→    fun countByReviewer_Id(reviewerId: Long): Long
    51→
    52→    /**
    53→     * Count total reviews for a session.
    54→     */
    55→    fun countBySession_Id(sessionId: Long): Long
    56→
    57→    /**
    58→     * Get assessment summary for a snapshot.
    59→     * Returns counts of OK, CHANGE, NOGO assessments.
    60→     */
    61→    @Query("""
    62→        SELECT new map(
    63→            r.assessment as assessment,
    64→            COUNT(r) as count
    65→        )
    66→        FROM RequirementReview r
    67→        WHERE r.snapshot.id = :snapshotId
    68→        GROUP BY r.assessment
    69→    """)
    70→    fun getAssessmentSummary(snapshotId: Long): List<Map<String, Any>>
    71→
    72→    /**
    73→     * Find reviews with NOK assessment for a session.
    74→     */
    75→    @Query("SELECT r FROM RequirementReview r WHERE r.session.id = :sessionId AND r.assessment = 'NOK'")
    76→    fun findNokReviews(sessionId: Long): List<RequirementReview>
    77→
    78→    /**
    79→     * Delete all reviews for a session.
    80→     */
    81→    fun deleteBySession_Id(sessionId: Long)
    82→
    83→    /**
    84→     * Delete all reviews by a specific reviewer.
    85→     */
    86→    fun deleteByReviewer_Id(reviewerId: Long)
    87→}

    35→
    36→@Controller("/api/assets")
    37→@Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
    38→@ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.BLOCKING)
    39→open class AssetController(
    40→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    41→    private val demandRepository: DemandRepository,
    42→    private val riskAssessmentRepository: RiskAssessmentRepository,
    43→    private val riskRepository: RiskRepository,
    44→    private val entityManager: EntityManager,
    45→    private val scanResultRepository: ScanResultRepository,
    46→    private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository,
    47→    private val assetFilterService: AssetFilterService,
    48→    private val userRepository: UserRepository,
    49→    private val workgroupRepository: com.secman.repository.WorkgroupRepository,
    50→    private val assetBulkDeleteService: com.secman.service.AssetBulkDeleteService,
    51→    private val assetExportService: com.secman.service.AssetExportService,
    52→    private val assetCascadeDeleteService: com.secman.service.AssetCascadeDeleteService
    53→) {
    54→    
    55→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AssetController::class.java)
    56→
    57→    @Serdeable
    58→    data class CreateAssetRequest(
    59→        @NotBlank @Size(max = 255) val name: String,
    60→        @NotBlank val type: String,
    61→        @Nullable val ip: String? = null,
    62→        @NotBlank @Size(max = 255) val owner: String,
    63→        @Nullable val description: String? = null,
    64→        @Nullable val criticality: Criticality? = null,
    65→        @Nullable val adDomain: String? = null
    66→    )
    67→
    68→    @Serdeable
    69→    data class UpdateAssetRequest(
    70→        @Nullable val name: String? = null,
    71→        @Nullable val type: String? = null,
    72→        @Nullable val ip: String? = null,
    73→        @Nullable val owner: String? = null,
    74→        @Nullable val description: String? = null,
    75→        @Nullable val workgroupIds: List<Long>? = null,
    76→        @Nullable val criticality: Criticality? = null,
    77→        @Nullable val adDomain: String? = null
    78→    )
    79→
    80→    @Serdeable
    81→    data class ErrorResponse(
    82→        val error: String
    83→    )
    84→
    85→    /**
    86→     * List assets accessible to the authenticated user
    87→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
    88→     *
    89→     * FR-013, FR-016, FR-017: Filter by workgroup membership + ownership
    90→     * ADMIN sees all, regular users and VULN see their workgroup assets + owned assets
    91→     */
    92→    @Get
    93→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
    94→    open fun list(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<List<Asset>> {
    95→        return try {
    96→            log.debug("Fetching accessible assets for user: {}", authentication.name)
    97→
    98→            // Use AssetFilterService for workgroup-based filtering
    99→            val assets = assetFilterService.getAccessibleAssets(authentication)
   100→                .sortedByDescending { it.createdAt }

1→package com.secman.repository
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.AlignmentSnapshot
     4→import com.secman.domain.AlignmentSnapshot.ChangeType
     5→import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query
     6→import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Repository
     7→import io.micronaut.data.jpa.repository.JpaRepository
     8→import io.micronaut.data.model.Page
     9→import io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable
    10→
    11→/**
    12→ * Repository for AlignmentSnapshot entity.
    13→ * Feature: 068-requirements-alignment-process
    14→ */
    15→@Repository
    16→interface AlignmentSnapshotRepository : JpaRepository<AlignmentSnapshot, Long> {
    17→
    18→    /**
    19→     * Find all snapshots for a session.
    20→     */
    21→    fun findBySession_Id(sessionId: Long): List<AlignmentSnapshot>
    22→
    23→    /**
    24→     * Find all snapshots for a session with pagination.
    25→     */
    26→    fun findBySession_Id(sessionId: Long, pageable: Pageable): Page<AlignmentSnapshot>
    27→
    28→    /**
    29→     * Find snapshots by change type for a session.
    30→     */
    31→    fun findBySession_IdAndChangeType(sessionId: Long, changeType: ChangeType): List<AlignmentSnapshot>
    32→
    33→    /**
    34→     * Find snapshot by session and requirement internal ID.
    35→     */
    36→    fun findBySession_IdAndRequirementInternalId(sessionId: Long, requirementInternalId: String): AlignmentSnapshot?
    37→
    38→    /**
    39→     * Count snapshots by change type for a session.
    40→     */
    41→    fun countBySession_IdAndChangeType(sessionId: Long, changeType: ChangeType): Long
    42→
    43→    /**
    44→     * Count total snapshots in a session.
    45→     */
    46→    fun countBySession_Id(sessionId: Long): Long
    47→
    48→    /**
    49→     * Get change type summary for a session.
    50→     */
    51→    @Query("""
    52→        SELECT new map(
    53→            s.changeType as changeType,
    54→            COUNT(s) as count
    55→        )
    56→        FROM AlignmentSnapshot s
    57→        WHERE s.session.id = :sessionId
    58→        GROUP BY s.changeType
    59→    """)
    60→    fun getChangeTypeSummary(sessionId: Long): List<Map<String, Any>>
    61→
    62→    /**
    63→     * Delete all snapshots for a session.
    64→     */
    65→    fun deleteBySession_Id(sessionId: Long)
    66→
    67→    /**
    68→     * Find all added requirements in a session.
    69→     */
    70→    @Query("SELECT s FROM AlignmentSnapshot s WHERE s.session.id = :sessionId AND s.changeType = 'ADDED' ORDER BY s.requirementInternalId")
    71→    fun findAddedRequirements(sessionId: Long): List<AlignmentSnapshot>
    72→
    73→    /**
    74→     * Find all modified requirements in a session.
    75→     */
    76→    @Query("SELECT s FROM AlignmentSnapshot s WHERE s.session.id = :sessionId AND s.changeType = 'MODIFIED' ORDER BY s.requirementInternalId")
    77→    fun findModifiedRequirements(sessionId: Long): List<AlignmentSnapshot>
    78→
    79→    /**
    80→     * Find all deleted requirements in a session.
    81→     */
    82→    @Query("SELECT s FROM AlignmentSnapshot s WHERE s.session.id = :sessionId AND s.changeType = 'DELETED' ORDER BY s.requirementInternalId")
    83→    fun findDeletedRequirements(sessionId: Long): List<AlignmentSnapshot>
    84→}
    85→

150→    @Transactional
   151→    open fun create(@Valid @Body request: CreateAssetRequest, authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   152→        return try {
   153→            log.debug("Creating asset with name: {} for user: {}", request.name, authentication.name)
   154→
   155→            // Validate required fields are not blank after trimming
   156→            val trimmedName = request.name.trim()
   157→            val trimmedType = request.type.trim()
   158→            val trimmedOwner = request.owner.trim()
   159→
   160→            if (trimmedName.isBlank()) {
   161→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Name cannot be empty"))
   162→            }
   163→
   164→            if (trimmedType.isBlank()) {
   165→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Type cannot be empty"))
   166→            }
   167→
   168→            if (trimmedOwner.isBlank()) {
   169→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Owner cannot be empty"))
   170→            }
   171→
   172→            // Get user ID from authentication for manual creator tracking
   173→            val userId = authentication.attributes["userId"]?.toString()?.toLongOrNull()
   174→            val manualCreator = if (userId != null) {
   175→                userRepository.findById(userId).orElse(null)
   176→            } else {
   177→                null
   178→            }
   179→
   180→            // Create new asset with manual creator tracking
   181→            val asset = Asset(
   182→                name = trimmedName,
   183→                type = trimmedType,
   184→                ip = request.ip?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   185→                owner = trimmedOwner,
   186→                description = request.description?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   187→                criticality = request.criticality,
   188→                manualCreator = manualCreator
   189→            )
   190→            asset.adDomain = request.adDomain?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   191→
   192→            val savedAsset = assetRepository.save(asset)
   193→
   194→            log.info("Created asset: {} with id: {} by user: {}", savedAsset.name, savedAsset.id, authentication.name)
   195→            HttpResponse.status<Asset>(HttpStatus.CREATED).body(savedAsset)
   196→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   197→            log.error("Error creating asset", e)
   198→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Error creating asset: ${e.message}"))
   199→        }
   200→    }
   201→
   202→    @Put("/{id}")
   203→    @Transactional
   204→    open fun update(id: Long, @Valid @Body request: UpdateAssetRequest): HttpResponse<*> {
   205→        return try {
   206→            log.debug("Updating asset with id: {}", id)
   207→            
   208→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   209→                ?: return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("Asset not found"))
   210→            
   211→            // Update fields if provided (partial update support like Java implementation)
   212→            request.name?.let { newName ->
   213→                val trimmedName = newName.trim()
   214→                if (trimmedName.isBlank()) {
   215→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Name cannot be empty"))
   216→                }
   217→                asset.name = trimmedName
   218→            }
   219→            
   220→            request.type?.let { newType ->
   221→                val trimmedType = newType.trim()
   222→                if (trimmedType.isBlank()) {
   223→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Type cannot be empty"))
   224→                }
   225→                asset.type = trimmedType
   226→            }
   227→            
   228→            request.ip?.let { newIp ->
   229→                asset.ip = newIp.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   230→            }
   231→            
   232→            request.owner?.let { newOwner ->
   233→                val trimmedOwner = newOwner.trim()
   234→                if (trimmedOwner.isBlank()) {
   235→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Owner cannot be empty"))
   236→                }
   237→                asset.owner = trimmedOwner
   238→            }
   239→            
   240→            request.description?.let { newDescription ->
   241→                asset.description = newDescription.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   242→            }
   243→
   244→            // Feature 039: Handle criticality update (null explicitly allowed to revert to inheritance)
   245→            if (request.criticality !== null) {
   246→                asset.criticality = request.criticality
   247→            }
   248→
   249→            // Feature 053: Handle adDomain update
   250→            request.adDomain?.let { newAdDomain ->
   251→                asset.adDomain = newAdDomain.trim().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   252→            }
   253→
   254→            request.workgroupIds?.let { workgroupIds ->
   255→                val workgroups = workgroupIds.mapNotNull { id ->
   256→                    workgroupRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   257→                }
   258→
   259→                if (workgroups.size != workgroupIds.size) {
   260→                    return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("One or more workgroup IDs not found"))
   261→                }
   262→
   263→                // Update workgroup assignments
   264→                asset.workgroups.clear()
   265→                asset.workgroups.addAll(workgroups)
   266→            }
   267→
   268→            val updatedAsset = assetRepository.update(asset)
   269→
   270→            log.info("Updated asset: {} with id: {}", updatedAsset.name, updatedAsset.id)
   271→            HttpResponse.ok(updatedAsset)
   272→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   273→            log.error("Error updating asset with id: {}", id, e)
   274→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Error updating asset: ${e.message}"))
   275→        }
   276→    }
   277→
   278→    /**
   279→     * Get cascade deletion summary for pre-flight validation
   280→     * Feature: 033-cascade-asset-deletion (User Story 4 - UI Warning Before Cascade Deletion)
   281→     *
   282→     * GET /api/assets/{id}/cascade-summary
   283→     * Auth: Any authenticated user (ADMIN recommended)
   284→     * Response: CascadeDeleteSummaryDto
   285→     *
   286→     * Related Requirements:
   287→     * - FR-012: System MUST perform a pre-flight count of related records
   288→     * - Contract: contracts/cascade-delete-api.yaml
   289→     *
   290→     * Error Responses:
   291→     * - 404: Asset not found
   292→     * - 500: Internal server error
   293→     */
   294→    @Get("/{id}/cascade-summary")
   295→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   296→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
   297→    open fun getCascadeSummary(id: Long): HttpResponse<*> {
   298→        return try {
   299→            log.debug("Getting cascade summary for asset id: {}", id)
   300→
   301→            val summary = assetCascadeDeleteService.getCascadeSummary(id)
   302→
   303→            log.info("Cascade summary for asset {}: {} vulns, {} exceptions, {} requests, estimated {}s",
   304→                id, summary.vulnerabilitiesCount, summary.assetExceptionsCount,
   305→                summary.exceptionRequestsCount, summary.estimatedDurationSeconds)
   306→
   307→            HttpResponse.ok(summary)
   308→
   309→        } catch (e: AssetCascadeDeleteService.AssetNotFoundException) {
   310→            log.warn("Asset not found for cascade summary: {}", id)
   311→            HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse(e.message ?: "Asset not found"))
   312→
   313→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   314→            log.error("Error getting cascade summary for asset: {}", id, e)
   315→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   316→                .body(ErrorResponse("Failed to get cascade summary: ${e.message}"))
   317→        }
   318→    }
   319→
   320→    /**
   321→     * Delete asset with cascade deletion of all related data
   322→     * Feature: 033-cascade-asset-deletion (User Story 1 - Delete Asset with All Related Data)
   323→     *
   324→     * DELETE /api/assets/{id}
   325→     * Auth: ADMIN role required
   326→     * Response: CascadeDeletionResultDto
   327→     *
   328→     * Related Requirements:
   329→     * - FR-001: System MUST cascade delete vulnerabilities when asset is deleted
   330→     * - FR-002: System MUST cascade delete ASSET-type exceptions
   331→     * - FR-003: System MUST cascade delete vulnerability exception requests
   332→     * - FR-011: Use pessimistic row-level locking to prevent concurrent deletion
   333→     * - FR-013: Provide detailed structured error messages
   334→     * - Contract: contracts/cascade-delete-api.yaml
   335→     *
   336→     * Error Responses:
   337→     * - 403: User does not have ADMIN role
   338→     * - 404: Asset not found
   339→     * - 409: Asset is locked by another transaction (concurrent deletion)
   340→     * - 422: Deletion would exceed timeout (forceTimeout parameter can override)
   341→     * - 500: Internal server error or transaction timeout
   342→     */
   343→    @Delete("/{id}")
   344→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   345→    open fun delete(
   346→        id: Long,
   347→        @QueryValue(defaultValue = "false") forceTimeout: Boolean,
   348→        authentication: Authentication
   349→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   350→        return try {
   351→            log.info("Cascade delete request for asset {} by user {} (forceTimeout={})",
   352→                id, authentication.name, forceTimeout)
   353→
   354→            // Check for non-cascade references before deletion (demands, risk assessments, risks)
   355→            val referencingDemands = demandRepository.findByExistingAssetId(id)
   356→            val referencingRiskAssessments = riskAssessmentRepository.findAllByInvolvedAssetId(id)
   357→            val referencingRisks = riskRepository.findByAssetId(id)
   358→
   359→            if (referencingDemands.isNotEmpty() || referencingRiskAssessments.isNotEmpty() || referencingRisks.isNotEmpty()) {
   360→                val errorMessages = mutableListOf<String>()
   361→
   362→                if (referencingDemands.isNotEmpty()) {
   363→                    errorMessages.add("${referencingDemands.size} demand(s) reference this asset")
   364→                }
   365→
   366→                if (referencingRiskAssessments.isNotEmpty()) {
   367→                    errorMessages.add("${referencingRiskAssessments.size} risk assessment(s) reference this asset")
   368→                }
   369→

   371→                    errorMessages.add("${referencingRisks.size} risk(s) reference this asset")
   372→                }
   373→
   374→                // Get asset name for error message
   375→                val asset = assetRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   376→                val assetName = asset?.name ?: "Asset #$id"
   377→
   378→                val detailedMessage = "Cannot delete asset '$assetName' - it is referenced by: ${errorMessages.joinToString(" and ")}. Please handle these references first."
   379→
   380→                log.warn("Asset deletion blocked for id: {} - {}", id, detailedMessage)
   381→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(detailedMessage))
   382→            }
   383→
   384→            // Perform cascade deletion
   385→            val result = assetCascadeDeleteService.deleteAsset(
   386→                assetId = id,
   387→                username = authentication.name,
   388→                forceTimeout = forceTimeout
   389→            )
   390→
   391→            log.info("Cascade delete successful for asset {}: {} vulns, {} exceptions, {} requests deleted",
   392→                id, result.deletedVulnerabilities, result.deletedExceptions, result.deletedRequests)
   393→
   394→            HttpResponse.ok(result)
   395→
   396→        } catch (e: AssetCascadeDeleteService.AssetNotFoundException) {
   397→            log.warn("Asset not found for deletion: {}", id)

Explore existing Excel export patterns in this Kotlin/Micronaut backend. I need to understand:

1. How Excel exports are currently implemented - look for Apache POI usage, SXSSFWorkbook, XSSFWorkbook
2. Any export service classes or utilities
3. How existing export endpoints return Excel files (content-type headers, response handling)
4. The pattern used for streaming exports vs in-memory exports

Search in:
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/service/ for any export-related services
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/controller/ for export endpoints
- build.gradle.kts files for Apache POI dependencies
- Look for patterns like "application/vnd.openxmlformats" or "xlsx" or "SXSSFWorkbook" or "XSSFWorkbook"

Be very thorough - I need to understand the exact patterns used so I can replicate them.

Be thorough - read the full component files to understand the UI structure and data flow.

400→        } catch (e: PessimisticLockException) {
   401→            log.warn("Asset {} is locked by another transaction", id)
   402→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   403→            val errorDto = assetCascadeDeleteService.buildLockedErrorDto(
   404→                assetId = id,
   405→                assetName = asset?.name ?: "Asset #$id",
   406→                cause = "Asset is currently being deleted by another user"
   407→            )
   408→            HttpResponse.status<DeletionErrorDto>(HttpStatus.CONFLICT).body(errorDto)
   409→
   410→        } catch (e: AssetCascadeDeleteService.TimeoutWarningException) {
   411→            log.warn("Asset {} deletion would exceed timeout: {}s", id, e.estimatedSeconds)
   412→            val errorDto = assetCascadeDeleteService.buildTimeoutWarningDto(
   413→                assetId = e.assetId,
   414→                assetName = e.assetName,
   415→                estimatedSeconds = e.estimatedSeconds
   416→            )
   417→            HttpResponse.status<DeletionErrorDto>(HttpStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY).body(errorDto)
   418→
   419→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   420→            log.error("Failed to delete asset {}", id, e)
   421→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   422→            val errorDto = assetCascadeDeleteService.buildInternalErrorDto(
   423→                assetId = id,
   424→                assetName = asset?.name ?: "Asset #$id",
   425→                cause = "Deletion failed due to internal error",
   426→                technicalDetails = e.message
   427→            )
   428→            HttpResponse.serverError<DeletionErrorDto>().body(errorDto)
   429→        }
   430→    }
   431→
   432→    /**
   433→     * Get port history for an asset
   434→     *
   435→     * GET /api/assets/{id}/ports
   436→     * Auth: Any authenticated user
   437→     * Response: PortHistoryDTO with scan history and port data
   438→     *
   439→     * Related to:
   440→     * - Feature: 002-implement-a-parsing (Nmap Scan Import)
   441→     * - Contract: specs/002-implement-a-parsing/contracts/asset-ports.yaml
   442→     * - FR-011: Display port scan history
   443→     */
   444→    @Get("/{id}/ports")
   445→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
   446→    open fun getPortHistory(id: Long): HttpResponse<*> {
   447→        return try {
   448→            log.debug("Fetching port history for asset id: {}", id)
   449→
   450→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   451→                ?: return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("Asset not found"))
   452→
   453→            // Get scan results ordered by discoveredAt DESC (newest first)
   454→            val scanResults = scanResultRepository.findByAssetIdOrderByDiscoveredAtDesc(id)
   455→
   456→            // Convert to DTOs
   457→            val scans = scanResults.map { result ->
   458→                val ports = result.ports.map { port ->
   459→                    PortDTO(
   460→                        portNumber = port.portNumber,
   461→                        protocol = port.protocol,
   462→                        state = port.state,
   463→                        service = port.service,
   464→                        version = port.version
   465→                    )
   466→                }
   467→
   468→                ScanPortsDTO(
   469→                    scanId = result.scan.id!!,
   470→                    scanDate = result.discoveredAt,
   471→                    scanType = result.scan.scanType,
   472→                    ports = ports
   473→                )
   474→            }
   475→
   476→            val response = PortHistoryDTO(
   477→                assetId = asset.id!!,
   478→                assetName = asset.name,
   479→                scans = scans
   480→            )
   481→
   482→            log.debug("Found {} scans for asset {}", scans.size, asset.name)
   483→            HttpResponse.ok(response)
   484→
   485→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   486→            log.error("Error fetching port history for asset id: {}", id, e)
   487→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>().body(ErrorResponse("Error fetching port history: ${e.message}"))
   488→        }
   489→    }
   490→
   491→    /**
   492→     * Get vulnerabilities for an asset with access control
   493→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
   494→     *
   495→     * GET /api/assets/{assetId}/vulnerabilities
   496→     * Auth: Any authenticated user
   497→     * Response: List<Vulnerability>
   498→     *
   499→     * Related to:
   500→     * - Feature: 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
   501→     * - Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
   502→     * - FR-021: Filter asset vulnerabilities by accessibility
   503→     */
   504→    @Get("/{assetId}/vulnerabilities")
   505→    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
   506→    open fun getVulnerabilities(
   507→        assetId: Long,
   508→        authentication: Authentication
   509→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   510→        return try {
   511→            log.debug("Fetching vulnerabilities for asset id: {} for user: {}", assetId, authentication.name)
   512→
   513→            // Use AssetFilterService for access-controlled vulnerability retrieval
   514→            val vulnerabilities = assetFilterService.getAssetVulnerabilities(assetId, authentication)
   515→
   516→            if (vulnerabilities.isEmpty()) {
   517→                // Could mean asset not found OR user doesn't have access
   518→                val asset = assetRepository.findById(assetId).orElse(null)
   519→                if (asset == null) {
   520→                    return HttpResponse.notFound(ErrorResponse("Asset not found"))
   521→                }
   522→                // Asset exists but user can't access it - return empty list
   523→                log.debug("User {} has no access to asset {}", authentication.name, assetId)
   524→            }
   525→
   526→            log.debug("Found {} vulnerabilities for asset {}", vulnerabilities.size, assetId)
   527→            HttpResponse.ok(vulnerabilities)
   528→
   529→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   530→            log.error("Error fetching vulnerabilities for asset id: {}", assetId, e)
   531→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>().body(ErrorResponse("Error fetching vulnerabilities: ${e.message}"))
   532→        }
   533→    }
   534→
   535→    /**
   536→     * Export assets to Excel file
   537→     * Feature: 029-asset-bulk-operations (User Story 2 - Export Assets to File)
   538→     *
   539→     * GET /api/assets/export
   540→     * Auth: Any authenticated user
   541→     * Response: Binary Excel file (.xlsx)
   542→     *
   543→     * Related Requirements:
   544→     * - FR-010: Export assets to Excel with all fields
   545→     * - FR-011: Apply workgroup-based access control (ADMIN sees all, non-ADMIN sees workgroup+owned)
   546→     * - FR-012: Format export file with clear column headers
   547→     * - FR-014: Provide user feedback during export (loading indicators handled by frontend)
   548→     * - FR-015: Display error when no assets available
   549→     *
   550→     * Error Responses:
   551→     * - 400: No assets available to export
   552→     * - 401: User not authenticated
   553→     * - 500: Export failed
   554→     */
   555→    @Get("/export")
   556→    open fun exportAssets(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   557→        return try {
   558→            log.info("Asset export request from user: {}", authentication.name)
   559→
   560→            // Get assets with workgroup filtering
   561→            val dtos = assetExportService.exportAssets(authentication)
   562→
   563→            // Check if any assets are available
   564→            if (dtos.isEmpty()) {
   565→                log.warn("No assets available to export for user: {}", authentication.name)
   566→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("No assets available to export"))
   567→            }
   568→
   569→            // Write to Excel
   570→            val outputStream = assetExportService.writeToExcel(dtos)
   571→
   572→            // Generate filename with current date
   573→            val dateStr = java.time.LocalDate.now().toString()
   574→            val filename = "assets_export_$dateStr.xlsx"
   575→
   576→            log.info("Asset export successful: {} assets exported for user {}", dtos.size, authentication.name)
   577→
   578→            // Return binary Excel file with proper headers
   579→            HttpResponse.ok(outputStream.toByteArray())
   580→                .header("Content-Type", "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet")
   581→                .header("Content-Disposition", "attachment; filename=\"$filename\"")
   582→
   583→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   584→            log.error("Asset export failed for user: {}", authentication.name, e)
   585→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   586→                .body(ErrorResponse("Failed to export assets: ${e.message}"))
   587→        }
   588→    }
   589→
   590→    /**
   591→     * Bulk delete all assets (ADMIN only)
   592→     * Feature: 029-asset-bulk-operations (User Story 1 - Bulk Delete Assets)
   593→     *
   594→     * DELETE /api/assets/bulk
   595→     * Auth: ADMIN role required
   596→     * Response: BulkDeleteResult
   597→     *
   598→     * Related Requirements:
   599→     * - FR-001: Delete All Assets button visible only to ADMIN users
   600→     * - FR-003: Delete all assets when ADMIN confirms
   601→     * - FR-006: Prevent non-ADMIN users from accessing bulk delete
   602→     * - FR-007: Handle cascade deletion (vulnerabilities, scan results)
   603→     * - FR-008: Execute within transaction with rollback on failure
   604→     *
   605→     * Error Responses:
   606→     * - 403: User does not have ADMIN role
   607→     * - 409: Another bulk delete operation is in progress
   608→     * - 500: Transaction failed and was rolled back
   609→     */
   610→    @Delete("/bulk")
   611→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   612→    open fun bulkDeleteAssets(authentication: Authentication): HttpResponse<*> {
   613→        return try {
   614→            log.info("Bulk delete request received from user: {}", authentication.name)
   615→
   616→            val result = assetBulkDeleteService.deleteAllAssets()
   617→
   618→            log.info("Bulk delete successful: {}", result.message)
   619→            HttpResponse.ok(result)
   620→
   621→        } catch (e: com.secman.service.AssetBulkDeleteService.ConcurrentOperationException) {
   622→            log.warn("Concurrent bulk delete attempt rejected: {}", e.message)
   623→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.CONFLICT)
   624→                .body(ErrorResponse(e.message ?: "Bulk asset deletion already in progress"))
   625→
   626→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   627→            log.error("Bulk delete failed", e)
   628→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   629→                .body(ErrorResponse("Bulk delete failed. No assets were deleted."))
   630→        }
   631→    }
   632→}
